# Managing user access - Galaxy NG

 

[Skip to content](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/en/latest/config/user_access.html#user-authentication-and-access)

[](https://github.com/ansible/galaxy_ng/edit/main/docs/config/user_access.md "Edit this page")
[](https://github.com/ansible/galaxy_ng/raw/main/docs/config/user_access.md "View source of this page")

User Authentication and Access[¶](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/en/latest/config/user_access.html#user-authentication-and-access "Permanent link")

=================================================================================================================================================================

Configuring Minimum Password Length[¶](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/en/latest/config/user_access.html#configuring-minimum-password-length "Permanent link")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Info

This setting is valid for internal Django users only, for keycloak users the rules must be configured on the SSo service.

When creating new users on internal database (Django Users) a validation is performed and one can configure the minimum password length.

The default value is 9 and can be customized using:

/etc/pulp/settings.py

`[](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/en/latest/config/user_access.html#__codelineno-0-1) GALAXY_MINIMUM_PASSWORD_LENGTH=15`

envronment variables

`[](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/en/latest/config/user_access.html#__codelineno-1-1) export PULP_GALAXY_MINIMUM_PASSWORD_LENGTH=15`

Auto logout after set period of time[¶](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/en/latest/config/user_access.html#auto-logout-after-set-period-of-time "Permanent link")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Auto logout is configurable in Django by the `SESSION_COOKIE_AGE` variable

The default value is `1209600` (2 weeks, in seconds)

This default value can be changed, for exemple, to make session expire after 1 hour.

/etc/pulp/settings.py

`[](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/en/latest/config/user_access.html#__codelineno-2-1) SESSION_COOKIE_AGE=3600`

environment variables

`[](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/en/latest/config/user_access.html#__codelineno-3-1) export PULP_SESSION_COOKIE_AGE=3600`

Back to top

---

# Writing documentation - Galaxy NG

 

[Skip to content](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/en/latest/dev/writing_docs.html#writing-docs)

[](https://github.com/ansible/galaxy_ng/edit/main/docs/dev/writing_docs.md "Edit this page")
[](https://github.com/ansible/galaxy_ng/raw/main/docs/dev/writing_docs.md "View source of this page")

Writing Docs[¶](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/en/latest/dev/writing_docs.html#writing-docs "Permanent link")

===========================================================================================================================

Documentation is stored under the docs `docs/` directory in the [backend repository](https://github.com/ansible/galaxy_ng)
. Documentation built using [MkDocs](https://www.mkdocs.org/)
. Documentation is written using [Markdown](https://www.mkdocs.org/user-guide/writing-your-docs/#writing-with-markdown)
. Check out the [MkDocs documentation](https://www.mkdocs.org/getting-started/)
 for more information.

Building the Docs Site Locally[¶](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/en/latest/dev/writing_docs.html#building-the-docs-site-locally "Permanent link")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

1.  Install the docs dependencies
    
    Important
    
    We recommend creating a [python virtual environment](https://docs.python.org/3/library/venv.html)
     to install the documentation requirements in.
    
    `[](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/en/latest/dev/writing_docs.html#__codelineno-0-1) make docs/install`
    
2.  Run the documentation service.
    
    `[](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/en/latest/dev/writing_docs.html#__codelineno-1-1) make docs/serve`
    
3.  Navigate to [http://localhost:8000](http://localhost:8000/)
     to view the docs site.
    

Adding new pages[¶](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/en/latest/dev/writing_docs.html#adding-new-pages "Permanent link")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

1.  Create a new markdown file under `docs/`
2.  Add your new file under the "nav" section in `mkdocs.yml` in the project root directory.

Back to top

# Ansible Compat Library

 

[Skip to content](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/compat/#examples)

[](https://github.com/ansible/ansible-compat/blob/main/docs/index.md "Edit this page")

Examples[¶](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/compat/#examples "Permanent link")

=================================================================================

Using Ansible runtime[¶](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/compat/#using-ansible-runtime "Permanent link")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

example.py

`[](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/compat/#__codelineno-0-1) """Sample use of Runtime class.""" [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/compat/#__codelineno-0-2) [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/compat/#__codelineno-0-3) from ansible_compat.runtime import Runtime [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/compat/#__codelineno-0-4) [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/compat/#__codelineno-0-5) [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/compat/#__codelineno-0-6) def test_runtime_example() -> None: [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/compat/#__codelineno-0-7)     """Test basic functionality of Runtime class.""" [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/compat/#__codelineno-0-8)     # instantiate the runtime using isolated mode, so installing new [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/compat/#__codelineno-0-9)     # roles/collections do not pollute the default setup. [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/compat/#__codelineno-0-10)     runtime = Runtime(isolated=True, max_retries=3) [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/compat/#__codelineno-0-11) [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/compat/#__codelineno-0-12)     # Print Ansible core version [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/compat/#__codelineno-0-13)     _ = runtime.version  # 2.9.10 (Version object) [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/compat/#__codelineno-0-14)     # Get configuration info from runtime [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/compat/#__codelineno-0-15)     _ = runtime.config.collections_path [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/compat/#__codelineno-0-16) [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/compat/#__codelineno-0-17)     # Detect if current project is a collection and install its requirements [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/compat/#__codelineno-0-18)     runtime.prepare_environment(install_local=True)  # will retry 3 times if needed [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/compat/#__codelineno-0-19) [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/compat/#__codelineno-0-20)     # Install a new collection (will retry 3 times if needed) [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/compat/#__codelineno-0-21)     runtime.install_collection("examples/reqs_v2/community-molecule-0.1.0.tar.gz") [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/compat/#__codelineno-0-22) [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/compat/#__codelineno-0-23)     # Execute a command [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/compat/#__codelineno-0-24)     result = runtime.run(["ansible-doc", "--list"]) [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/compat/#__codelineno-0-25)     assert result.returncode == 0`

Access to Ansible configuration[¶](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/compat/#access-to-ansible-configuration "Permanent link")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

As you may not want to parse `ansible-config dump` yourself, you can make use of a simple python class that facilitates access to it, using python data types.

`[](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/compat/#__codelineno-1-1) """Sample usage of AnsibleConfig.""" [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/compat/#__codelineno-1-2) [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/compat/#__codelineno-1-3) from ansible_compat.config import AnsibleConfig [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/compat/#__codelineno-1-4) [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/compat/#__codelineno-1-5) [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/compat/#__codelineno-1-6) def test_example_config() -> None: [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/compat/#__codelineno-1-7)     """Test basic functionality of AnsibleConfig.""" [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/compat/#__codelineno-1-8)     cfg = AnsibleConfig() [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/compat/#__codelineno-1-9)     assert isinstance(cfg.ACTION_WARNINGS, bool) [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/compat/#__codelineno-1-10)     # you can also use lowercase: [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/compat/#__codelineno-1-11)     assert isinstance(cfg.action_warnings, bool) [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/compat/#__codelineno-1-12)     # you can also use it as dictionary [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/compat/#__codelineno-1-13)     assert cfg["action_warnings"] == cfg.action_warnings`

# Feature Flags - Galaxy NG

 

[Skip to content](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/en/latest/config/featureflags.html#feature-flags)

[](https://github.com/ansible/galaxy_ng/edit/main/docs/config/featureflags.md "Edit this page")
[](https://github.com/ansible/galaxy_ng/raw/main/docs/config/featureflags.md "View source of this page")

Feature Flags[¶](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/en/latest/config/featureflags.html#feature-flags "Permanent link")

================================================================================================================================

Some features are disabled by default. You can enable them by setting the feature flag under `GALAXY_FEATURE_FLAGS` setting.

Example for enabling EEs endpoints:

`via environment variables`

`[](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/en/latest/config/featureflags.html#__codelineno-0-1) export PULP_GALAXY_FEATURE_FLAGS__execution_environments=true`

`via /etc/pulp/settings.py`

`[](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/en/latest/config/featureflags.html#__codelineno-1-1) GALAXY_FEATURE_FLAGS__execution_environments = True [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/en/latest/config/featureflags.html#__codelineno-1-2) [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/en/latest/config/featureflags.html#__codelineno-1-3) # Alternatively [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/en/latest/config/featureflags.html#__codelineno-1-4) GALAXY_FEATURE_FLAGS = { [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/en/latest/config/featureflags.html#__codelineno-1-5)   "dynaconf_merge": True, [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/en/latest/config/featureflags.html#__codelineno-1-6)   "execution_environments": True [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/en/latest/config/featureflags.html#__codelineno-1-7) }`

The same pattern can be used for the following feature flags:

*   `signatures_enabled`
    *   boolean
    *   Enable the signing feature
    *   default: False (or turned true whenever a Signing Service is set)
*   `require_upload_signatures`
    *   boolean
    *   Require a signature to be upload before collection approval
    *   default: value of GALAXY\_REQUIRE\_SIGNATURE\_FOR\_APPROVAL setting.
*   `can_create_signatures`
    *   boolean
    *   Tells UI to show the signing buttons
    *   default: signatures\_enabled AND Signing Service is set AND keys present
*   `can_upload_signatures`
    *   boolean
    *   Tells UI to show the upload signature button
    *   default: signatures\_enabled AND GALAXY\_REQUIRE\_SIGNATURE\_FOR\_APPROVAL
*   `collection_auto_sign`
    *   boolean
    *   Set the signature to be created automatically when collection is approved
    *   Default: value of GALAXY\_AUTO\_SIGN\_COLLECTIONS setting.
*   `display_signatures`
    *   boolean
    *   Tells UI to show the signature information on badges
    *   default: signatures\_enabled AND (can create or can upload)
*   `execution_environments`
    *   boolean
    *   Tells UI to show the execution environments tab and enable EE endpoints
    *   default: False
*   `container_signing`
    *   boolean
    *   Tells UI to show the container signing buttons and badges
    *   default: True when Container Signing Service is configured
*   `ai_deny_index`
    *   boolean
    *   Enables AI Deny Index endpoints and tells UI to display the Wisdom opt-out buttons
    *   default: False
*   `display_repositories`
    *   boolean
    *   Signals the UI to show or not to show repository information.
    *   default: True

Back to top

---

# Debugging the AWX Operator - Ansible AWX Operator Documentation

 

[Skip to content](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/awx-operator/en/latest/troubleshooting/debugging.html#debugging-the-awx-operator)

[](https://github.com/ansible/awx-operator/blob/devel/docs/troubleshooting/debugging.md "Edit this page")

Debugging the AWX Operator[¶](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/awx-operator/en/latest/troubleshooting/debugging.html#debugging-the-awx-operator "Permanent link")

===================================================================================================================================================================

General Debugging[¶](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/awx-operator/en/latest/troubleshooting/debugging.html#general-debugging "Permanent link")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

When the operator is deploying AWX, it is running the `installer` role inside the operator container. If the AWX CR's status is `Failed`, it is often useful to look at the awx-operator container logs, which shows the output of the installer role. To see these logs, run:

`[](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/awx-operator/en/latest/troubleshooting/debugging.html#__codelineno-0-1) kubectl logs deployments/awx-operator-controller-manager -c awx-manager -f`

### Inspect k8s Resources[¶](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/awx-operator/en/latest/troubleshooting/debugging.html#inspect-k8s-resources "Permanent link")

Past that, it is often useful to inspect various resources the AWX Operator manages like:

*   awx
*   awxbackup
*   awxrestore
*   pod
*   deployment
*   pvc
*   service
*   ingress
*   route
*   secrets
*   serviceaccount

To inspect these resources you can use these commands

`[](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/awx-operator/en/latest/troubleshooting/debugging.html#__codelineno-1-1) # Inspecting k8s resources [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/awx-operator/en/latest/troubleshooting/debugging.html#__codelineno-1-2) kubectl describe -n <namespace> <resource> <resource-name> [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/awx-operator/en/latest/troubleshooting/debugging.html#__codelineno-1-3) kubectl get -n <namespace> <resource> <resource-name> -o yaml [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/awx-operator/en/latest/troubleshooting/debugging.html#__codelineno-1-4) kubectl logs -n <namespace> <resource> <resource-name> [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/awx-operator/en/latest/troubleshooting/debugging.html#__codelineno-1-5) [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/awx-operator/en/latest/troubleshooting/debugging.html#__codelineno-1-6) # Inspecting Pods [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/awx-operator/en/latest/troubleshooting/debugging.html#__codelineno-1-7) kubectl exec -it -n <namespace> <pod> <pod-name>`

### Configure No Log[¶](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/awx-operator/en/latest/troubleshooting/debugging.html#configure-no-log "Permanent link")

It is possible to show task output for debugging by setting no\_log to false on the AWX CR spec. This will show output in the awx-operator logs for any failed tasks where no\_log was set to true.

For example:

`[](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/awx-operator/en/latest/troubleshooting/debugging.html#__codelineno-2-1) --- [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/awx-operator/en/latest/troubleshooting/debugging.html#__codelineno-2-2) apiVersion: awx.ansible.com/v1beta1 [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/awx-operator/en/latest/troubleshooting/debugging.html#__codelineno-2-3) kind: AWX [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/awx-operator/en/latest/troubleshooting/debugging.html#__codelineno-2-4) metadata: [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/awx-operator/en/latest/troubleshooting/debugging.html#__codelineno-2-5)   name: awx-demo [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/awx-operator/en/latest/troubleshooting/debugging.html#__codelineno-2-6) spec: [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/awx-operator/en/latest/troubleshooting/debugging.html#__codelineno-2-7)   service_type: nodeport [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/awx-operator/en/latest/troubleshooting/debugging.html#__codelineno-2-8)   no_log: false                  # <------------`

Iterating on the installer without deploying the operator[¶](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/awx-operator/en/latest/troubleshooting/debugging.html#iterating-on-the-installer-without-deploying-the-operator "Permanent link")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Go through the [normal basic install](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/awx-operator/en/latest/installation/basic-install.html)
 steps.

Install some dependencies:

`[](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/awx-operator/en/latest/troubleshooting/debugging.html#__codelineno-3-1) ansible-galaxy collection install -r molecule/requirements.yml [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/awx-operator/en/latest/troubleshooting/debugging.html#__codelineno-3-2) pip install -r molecule/requirements.txt`

To prevent the changes we're about to make from being overwritten, scale down any running instance of the operator:

`[](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/awx-operator/en/latest/troubleshooting/debugging.html#__codelineno-4-1) kubectl scale deployment awx-operator-controller-manager --replicas=0`

Create a playbook that invokes the installer role (the operator uses ansible-runner's role execution feature):

`[](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/awx-operator/en/latest/troubleshooting/debugging.html#__codelineno-5-1) # run.yml [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/awx-operator/en/latest/troubleshooting/debugging.html#__codelineno-5-2) --- [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/awx-operator/en/latest/troubleshooting/debugging.html#__codelineno-5-3) - hosts: localhost [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/awx-operator/en/latest/troubleshooting/debugging.html#__codelineno-5-4)   roles: [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/awx-operator/en/latest/troubleshooting/debugging.html#__codelineno-5-5)     - installer`

Create a vars file:

`[](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/awx-operator/en/latest/troubleshooting/debugging.html#__codelineno-6-1) # vars.yml [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/awx-operator/en/latest/troubleshooting/debugging.html#__codelineno-6-2) --- [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/awx-operator/en/latest/troubleshooting/debugging.html#__codelineno-6-3) ansible_operator_meta: [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/awx-operator/en/latest/troubleshooting/debugging.html#__codelineno-6-4)   name: awx [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/awx-operator/en/latest/troubleshooting/debugging.html#__codelineno-6-5)   namespace: awx [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/awx-operator/en/latest/troubleshooting/debugging.html#__codelineno-6-6) set_self_labels: false [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/awx-operator/en/latest/troubleshooting/debugging.html#__codelineno-6-7) update_status: false [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/awx-operator/en/latest/troubleshooting/debugging.html#__codelineno-6-8) service_type: nodeport`

The vars file will replace the awx resource so any value that you wish to over ride using the awx resource, put in the vars file. For example, if you wish to use your own image, version and pull policy, you can specify it like below:

`[](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/awx-operator/en/latest/troubleshooting/debugging.html#__codelineno-7-1) # vars.yml [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/awx-operator/en/latest/troubleshooting/debugging.html#__codelineno-7-2) --- [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/awx-operator/en/latest/troubleshooting/debugging.html#__codelineno-7-3) ansible_operator_meta: [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/awx-operator/en/latest/troubleshooting/debugging.html#__codelineno-7-4)   name: awx [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/awx-operator/en/latest/troubleshooting/debugging.html#__codelineno-7-5)   namespace: awx [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/awx-operator/en/latest/troubleshooting/debugging.html#__codelineno-7-6) set_self_labels: false [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/awx-operator/en/latest/troubleshooting/debugging.html#__codelineno-7-7) update_status: false [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/awx-operator/en/latest/troubleshooting/debugging.html#__codelineno-7-8) service_type: nodeport [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/awx-operator/en/latest/troubleshooting/debugging.html#__codelineno-7-9) image: $DEV_DOCKER_TAG_BASE/awx_kube_devel [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/awx-operator/en/latest/troubleshooting/debugging.html#__codelineno-7-10) image_pull_policy: Always [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/awx-operator/en/latest/troubleshooting/debugging.html#__codelineno-7-11) image_version: $COMPOSE_TAG`

Run the installer:

`[](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/awx-operator/en/latest/troubleshooting/debugging.html#__codelineno-8-1) ansible-playbook run.yml -e @vars.yml -v`

Grab the URL and admin password:

`[](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/awx-operator/en/latest/troubleshooting/debugging.html#__codelineno-9-1) $ kubectl get secret awx-admin-password -- -o jsonpath="{.data.password}" | base64 --decode ; echo [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/awx-operator/en/latest/troubleshooting/debugging.html#__codelineno-9-2) LU6lTfvnkjUvDwL240kXKy1sNhjakZmT`

---

# Galaxy NG

 

[Skip to content](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/en/latest/#galaxy-ng)

[](https://github.com/ansible/galaxy_ng/edit/main/docs/index.md "Edit this page")
[](https://github.com/ansible/galaxy_ng/raw/main/docs/index.md "View source of this page")

![](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/en/latest/imgs/medium.png)

Warning

This documentation is a **Work In Progress** some pages are incomplete.

Galaxy NG[¶](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/en/latest/#galaxy-ng "Permanent link")

================================================================================================

![](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ansible/logos/be211ebccc316652eb725db688e75d932f8fa073/galaxy/galaxy-logo.svg)

[![Maintainability Rating](https://sonarcloud.io/api/project_badges/measure?project=ansible_galaxy_ng&metric=sqale_rating)](https://sonarcloud.io/summary/new_code?id=ansible_galaxy_ng)
 [![Build Status](https://github.com/ansible/galaxy_ng/actions/workflows/ci-docker-compose-integration.yml/badge.svg)](https://github.com/ansible/galaxy_ng/actions/workflows/ci-docker-compose-integration.yml)
 [![Coverage](https://sonarcloud.io/api/project_badges/measure?project=ansible_galaxy_ng&metric=coverage)](https://sonarcloud.io/summary/new_code?id=ansible_galaxy_ng)

**Source Code**: [https://github.com/ansible/galaxy\_ng](https://github.com/ansible/galaxy_ng)

A [Pulp](https://pulpproject.org/)
 plugin to support hosting your very own Ansible Galaxy server.

Our mission is to help organizations share Ansible automation and promote a culture of collaboration around Ansible automation development. We'll be providing features that make it easy to create, discover, use and distribute Ansible automation content.

For users[¶](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/en/latest/#for-users "Permanent link")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Check out our:

*   [Installation guide](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/en/latest/usage_guide/installation.html)
     to install Galaxy NG
*   [Collection guide](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/en/latest/usage_guide/collections.html)
     for information on managing collections
*   [Execution environment guide](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/en/latest/usage_guide/execution_environments.html)
     for information on managing execution environments
*   [RBAC and User management guide](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/en/latest/usage_guide/rbac.html)
     for information on setting up users and managing permissions

Back to top

# Administration - Galaxy NG

 

[Skip to content](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/en/latest/community/administration.html#community-galaxy-administration)

[](https://github.com/ansible/galaxy_ng/edit/main/docs/community/administration.md "Edit this page")
[](https://github.com/ansible/galaxy_ng/raw/main/docs/community/administration.md "View source of this page")

Community Galaxy Administration[¶](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/en/latest/community/administration.html#community-galaxy-administration "Permanent link")

=========================================================================================================================================================================

Creating a new v3 namespace[¶](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/en/latest/community/administration.html#creating-a-new-v3-namespace "Permanent link")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

V3 Namespaces can be created in one of two methods: API or WebUI.

### WebUI[¶](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/en/latest/community/administration.html#webui "Permanent link")

1) Login as a superuser 2) Expand "Collections" in the left nav 3) Click on "Namespaces" in the left nav 4) Click the "Create" button in the middle of the top header. 5) Fill in an appropriate name. 6) Click "Create" to save. 7) In the summary page for the new namespace, click on the 3 dot hamburger icon on the top right. 8) Choose "Edit Namespace" from the menu. 9) Fill in the various details such as the company name, logo url, and description. 10) Click "Save"

### API[¶](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/en/latest/community/administration.html#api "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/en/latest/community/administration.html#__codelineno-0-1) $ curl -X POST \ [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/en/latest/community/administration.html#__codelineno-0-2)     -H 'Authorization: token <TOKEN>' \ [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/en/latest/community/administration.html#__codelineno-0-3)     -H 'Content-Type: appliction/json' \ [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/en/latest/community/administration.html#__codelineno-0-4)     -d '{"name": "foobar", "groups": []}' \ [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/en/latest/community/administration.html#__codelineno-0-5)     https://galaxy-dev.ansible.com/api/_ui/v1/namespaces/`

TBD: Setting namespace details via the API.

Setting namespace owners[¶](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/en/latest/community/administration.html#setting-namespace-owners "Permanent link")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Social auth in galaxy\_ng has been heavily customized per [https://github.com/ansible/galaxy\_ng/pull/1881](https://github.com/ansible/galaxy_ng/pull/1881)

A "legacy" namespace is the api/v1 style namespace that allows any valid github username as the name.

A "v3" namespace is the api/v3 and pulp related namespace which is restricted to allowable python package names because of v3 being solely focused on ansible collections.

The legacy namespaces should have a foreign key relationship with a v3 namespace (which we'll call the "provider" namespace). The provider namespace is where owner management should occur.

A user on galaxy should be able to import roles into their legacy namespace, and also upload collections to their provider namespace (or any v3 namespace they've been added to). As the user logs into galaxy, the backend should validate and create their legacy namespace and the provider/v3 namespace automatically.

#### Validating and fixing a user via the django shell[¶](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/en/latest/community/administration.html#validating-and-fixing-a-user-via-the-django-shell "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/en/latest/community/administration.html#__codelineno-1-1) pulpcore-manager shell [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/en/latest/community/administration.html#__codelineno-1-2) from galaxy_ng.app.models import User [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/en/latest/community/administration.html#__codelineno-1-3) from galaxy_ng.app.api.v1.models import LegacyNamespace [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/en/latest/community/administration.html#__codelineno-1-4) from galaxy_ng.app.models import Namespace [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/en/latest/community/administration.html#__codelineno-1-5) from galaxy_ng.app.utils import rbac [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/en/latest/community/administration.html#__codelineno-1-6) from pulpcore.plugin.util import get_groups_with_perms_attached_roles [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/en/latest/community/administration.html#__codelineno-1-7) from pulpcore.plugin.util import get_users_with_perms_attached_roles [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/en/latest/community/administration.html#__codelineno-1-8) [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/en/latest/community/administration.html#__codelineno-1-9) # find the user ... [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/en/latest/community/administration.html#__codelineno-1-10) sean = User.objects.filter(username='sean-m-sullivan').first() [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/en/latest/community/administration.html#__codelineno-1-11) [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/en/latest/community/administration.html#__codelineno-1-12) # find the user's legacy namespace ... [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/en/latest/community/administration.html#__codelineno-1-13) legacy_namespace = LegacyNamespace.objects.filter(name='sean-m-sullivan').first() [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/en/latest/community/administration.html#__codelineno-1-14) assert legacy_namespace is not None [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/en/latest/community/administration.html#__codelineno-1-15) [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/en/latest/community/administration.html#__codelineno-1-16) # check the "provider" namespace of the legacynamespace ... [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/en/latest/community/administration.html#__codelineno-1-17) provider_namespace = legacy_namespace.namespace [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/en/latest/community/administration.html#__codelineno-1-18) assert provider_namespace is not None [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/en/latest/community/administration.html#__codelineno-1-19) [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/en/latest/community/administration.html#__codelineno-1-20) # get a list of owners for the provider namespace ... [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/en/latest/community/administration.html#__codelineno-1-21) owners = rbac.get_v3_namespace_owners(provider_namespace) [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/en/latest/community/administration.html#__codelineno-1-22) assert sean in owners`

To fix sean's RBAC on the sean-m-sullivan/sean\_m\_sullivan namespaces ...

`[](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/en/latest/community/administration.html#__codelineno-2-1) provider_namespace = Namespace.objects.filter(name='sean_m_sullivan').first() [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/en/latest/community/administration.html#__codelineno-2-2) rbac.add_user_to_v3_namespace(sean, provider_namespace) [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/en/latest/community/administration.html#__codelineno-2-3) owners = rbac.get_v3_namespace_owners(provider_namespace) [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/en/latest/community/administration.html#__codelineno-2-4) assert sean in owners [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/en/latest/community/administration.html#__codelineno-2-5) [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/en/latest/community/administration.html#__codelineno-2-6) legacy_namespace.namespace = provider_namespace [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/en/latest/community/administration.html#__codelineno-2-7) legacy_namespace.save()`

To fix sean's RBAC on the Wilk42/wilk42 namespaces ...

`[](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/en/latest/community/administration.html#__codelineno-3-1) legacy_namespace = LegacyNamespace.objects.filter(name='Wilk42').first() [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/en/latest/community/administration.html#__codelineno-3-2) # this legacy namespace already had the provider namespace of wilk42 [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/en/latest/community/administration.html#__codelineno-3-3) rbac.add_user_to_v3_namespace(sean, legacy_namespace.namespace) [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/en/latest/community/administration.html#__codelineno-3-4) owners = rbac.get_v3_namespace_owners(legacy_namespace.namespace) [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/en/latest/community/administration.html#__codelineno-3-5) assert sean in owners`

#### Validating and fixing a user via the API[¶](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/en/latest/community/administration.html#validating-and-fixing-a-user-via-the-api "Permanent link")

Find the legacy namespace ...

`[](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/en/latest/community/administration.html#__codelineno-4-1) curl https://galaxy-dev.ansible.com/api/v1/namespaces/?name=Wilk42 | jq .`

Check the provider namespace ...

`[](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/en/latest/community/administration.html#__codelineno-5-1) $ curl -s https://galaxy-dev.ansible.com/api/v1/namespaces/?name=Wilk42 | jq .results[0].summary_fields.provider_namespaces [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/en/latest/community/administration.html#__codelineno-5-2) [ [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/en/latest/community/administration.html#__codelineno-5-3)   { [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/en/latest/community/administration.html#__codelineno-5-4)     "id": 19193, [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/en/latest/community/administration.html#__codelineno-5-5)     "name": "wilk42", [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/en/latest/community/administration.html#__codelineno-5-6)     "pulp_href": "/api/pulp/api/v3/pulp_ansible/namespaces/19193/" [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/en/latest/community/administration.html#__codelineno-5-7)   } [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/en/latest/community/administration.html#__codelineno-5-8) ]`

Binding a provider namespace to the legacy namespace ...

`[](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/en/latest/community/administration.html#__codelineno-6-1) $ curl -X POST \ [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/en/latest/community/administration.html#__codelineno-6-2)     -H 'Authorization: token <TOKEN>' \ [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/en/latest/community/administration.html#__codelineno-6-3)     -H 'Content-Type: appliction/json' \ [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/en/latest/community/administration.html#__codelineno-6-4)     -d '{"id": 19192}' \ [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/en/latest/community/administration.html#__codelineno-6-5)     https://galaxy-dev.ansible.com/api/v1/namespaces/7532/providers/`

Check the owners ...

`[](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/en/latest/community/administration.html#__codelineno-7-1) $ curl -s https://galaxy-dev.ansible.com/api/v1/namespaces/?name=Wilk42 | jq .results[0].summary_fields. [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/en/latest/community/administration.html#__codelineno-7-2) owners [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/en/latest/community/administration.html#__codelineno-7-3) [ [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/en/latest/community/administration.html#__codelineno-7-4)   { [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/en/latest/community/administration.html#__codelineno-7-5)     "id": 7184, [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/en/latest/community/administration.html#__codelineno-7-6)     "username": "Wilk42" [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/en/latest/community/administration.html#__codelineno-7-7)   }, [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/en/latest/community/administration.html#__codelineno-7-8)   { [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/en/latest/community/administration.html#__codelineno-7-9)     "id": 17656, [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/en/latest/community/administration.html#__codelineno-7-10)     "username": "sean-m-sullivan" [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/en/latest/community/administration.html#__codelineno-7-11)   } [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/en/latest/community/administration.html#__codelineno-7-12) ]`

Setting the list of owners for a provider namespace ...

`[](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/en/latest/community/administration.html#__codelineno-8-1) $ curl -X POST \ [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/en/latest/community/administration.html#__codelineno-8-2)     -H 'Authorization: token <TOKEN>' \ [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/en/latest/community/administration.html#__codelineno-8-3)     -H 'Content-Type: appliction/json' \ [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/en/latest/community/administration.html#__codelineno-8-4)     -d '{"owners": [{"id": 7184}, {"id": 17656}]}' \ [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/en/latest/community/administration.html#__codelineno-8-5)     https://galaxy-dev.ansible.com/api/v1/namespaces/7532/owners/`

Deleting legacy roles[¶](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/en/latest/community/administration.html#deleting-legacy-roles "Permanent link")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Legacy role RBAC should allow superusers and legacy namespace owners to delete their owned roles ...

`[](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/en/latest/community/administration.html#__codelineno-9-1) $ curl -X DELETE \ [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/en/latest/community/administration.html#__codelineno-9-2)     -H 'Authorization: token <TOKEN>' \ [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/en/latest/community/administration.html#__codelineno-9-3)     https://galaxy-dev.ansible.com/api/v1/roles/<roleid>/`

The API does not currently support deleting a specific legacy role version.

Deleting collections[¶](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/en/latest/community/administration.html#deleting-collections "Permanent link")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Back to top

---

# Enabling Container signing - Galaxy NG

 

[Skip to content](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/en/latest/config/container_signing.html#enabling-container-signing)

[](https://github.com/ansible/galaxy_ng/edit/main/docs/config/container_signing.md "Edit this page")
[](https://github.com/ansible/galaxy_ng/raw/main/docs/config/container_signing.md "View source of this page")

Enabling Container Signing[¶](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/en/latest/config/container_signing.html#enabling-container-signing "Permanent link")

===============================================================================================================================================================

Galaxy as a container registry can handle container manifest signatures, the server can accept image push with signatures attached and can also create signatures on-demand via UI and API using a Pulp Signing Service.

Enabling signing on pulp installer

Pulp installer can also be configured to enable container signing [https://github.com/pulp/pulp\_installer/tree/main/roles/galaxy\_post\_install#variables-for-the-signing-service](https://github.com/pulp/pulp_installer/tree/main/roles/galaxy_post_install#variables-for-the-signing-service)
 if you configures using pulp-installer you can skip the `Creating Container Signing Service` section of this page.

### Pushing images with signatures[¶](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/en/latest/config/container_signing.html#pushing-images-with-signatures "Permanent link")

Pushing a tagged image altogether with its signature to the Galaxy Registry pass the `--sign-by` argument to the client.

`[](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/en/latest/config/container_signing.html#__codelineno-0-1) podman push --tls-verify=false --sign-by username@email.com localhost:5001`

### Signing Service[¶](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/en/latest/config/container_signing.html#signing-service "Permanent link")

A signing service is an object defined on the **pulp** backend that combines a GPG **key** and the absolute path of an executable script.

#### Creating a signing service[¶](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/en/latest/config/container_signing.html#creating-a-signing-service "Permanent link")

To create a signing service on the Galaxy server it is needed to access the `django-admin` utility and execute:

important

The command must run inside the Python Environment where galaxy\_ng workers are installed.

Bash Command

`[](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/en/latest/config/container_signing.html#__codelineno-1-1) django-admin add-signing-service \ #(1) [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/en/latest/config/container_signing.html#__codelineno-1-2)     unique-name \ #(2) [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/en/latest/config/container_signing.html#__codelineno-1-3)     /abs/path/to/script.sh \ #(3) [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/en/latest/config/container_signing.html#__codelineno-1-4)     GPG_KEY_ID #(4) [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/en/latest/config/container_signing.html#__codelineno-1-5)     --class container:ManifestSigningService #(5)`

1.  A django management command.
2.  Name the signing\_service should get in the database.
3.  Absolute path to Shell script where the signing is performed.
4.  Key id of the public key.
5.  For container it is needed to inform the `--class` argument

The positional arguments to the `add-signing-service` command:

*   **name**: Must be a unique name to identify the signing service object.
*   **script**: Must be a path to an executable acessible and runnable by the pulp worker process user. One example script can be found at the pulp documentation: [https://docs.pulpproject.org/pulpcore/workflows/signed-metadata.html#metadata-signing](https://docs.pulpproject.org/pulpcore/workflows/signed-metadata.html#metadata-signing)
    
*   **GPG\_KEY\_ID**: Must be the ID or e-mail that identifies the key on the global user keyring. The GPG key must be located at the pulp user level keyring and must be a valid GPG, there is a guide on how to create a valid gpg key here [https://access.redhat.com/articles/3359321](https://access.redhat.com/articles/3359321)
    

The named argument `--class`

*   `--class` needs to be set to value `container:ManifestSigningService` so additional checks can be performed.

Important

When importing the key to the keyring the trust level must be set to some value higher than 3. ex: `echo "${KEY_FINGERPRINT}:6:" | gpg --batch --import-ownertrust`

#### The signing script[¶](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/en/latest/config/container_signing.html#the-signing-script "Permanent link")

Pulp Container provides an example of a script that uses **skopeo** to produce signatures [https://docs.pulpproject.org/pulp\_container/workflows/sign-images.html#image-signature-configuration](https://docs.pulpproject.org/pulp_container/workflows/sign-images.html#image-signature-configuration)

/var/lib/pulp/scripts/container\_sign.sh

`[](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/en/latest/config/container_signing.html#__codelineno-2-1) #!/usr/bin/env bash [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/en/latest/config/container_signing.html#__codelineno-2-2) # This GPG_TTY variable might be needed on a container image that is not running as root. [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/en/latest/config/container_signing.html#__codelineno-2-3) # export GPG_TTY=$(tty) [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/en/latest/config/container_signing.html#__codelineno-2-4) [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/en/latest/config/container_signing.html#__codelineno-2-5) # pulp_container SigningService will pass the next 4 variables to the script. [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/en/latest/config/container_signing.html#__codelineno-2-6) MANIFEST_PATH=$1 [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/en/latest/config/container_signing.html#__codelineno-2-7) FINGERPRINT="$PULP_SIGNING_KEY_FINGERPRINT" [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/en/latest/config/container_signing.html#__codelineno-2-8) IMAGE_REFERENCE="$REFERENCE" [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/en/latest/config/container_signing.html#__codelineno-2-9) SIGNATURE_PATH="$SIG_PATH" [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/en/latest/config/container_signing.html#__codelineno-2-10) [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/en/latest/config/container_signing.html#__codelineno-2-11) # Create container signature using skopeo [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/en/latest/config/container_signing.html#__codelineno-2-12) skopeo standalone-sign \ [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/en/latest/config/container_signing.html#__codelineno-2-13)   $MANIFEST_PATH \ [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/en/latest/config/container_signing.html#__codelineno-2-14)   $IMAGE_REFERENCE \ [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/en/latest/config/container_signing.html#__codelineno-2-15)   $FINGERPRINT \ [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/en/latest/config/container_signing.html#__codelineno-2-16)   --output $SIGNATURE_PATH [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/en/latest/config/container_signing.html#__codelineno-2-17) [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/en/latest/config/container_signing.html#__codelineno-2-18) # Optionally pass the passphrase to the key if password protected. [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/en/latest/config/container_signing.html#__codelineno-2-19) # --passphrase-file /path/to/key_password.txt [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/en/latest/config/container_signing.html#__codelineno-2-20) [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/en/latest/config/container_signing.html#__codelineno-2-21) # Check the exit status [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/en/latest/config/container_signing.html#__codelineno-2-22) STATUS=$? [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/en/latest/config/container_signing.html#__codelineno-2-23) if [ $STATUS -eq 0 ]; then [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/en/latest/config/container_signing.html#__codelineno-2-24)   echo {\"signature_path\": \"$SIGNATURE_PATH\"} [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/en/latest/config/container_signing.html#__codelineno-2-25) else [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/en/latest/config/container_signing.html#__codelineno-2-26)   exit $STATUS [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/en/latest/config/container_signing.html#__codelineno-2-27) fi`

#### Using the key and script to create the signing service[¶](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/en/latest/config/container_signing.html#using-the-key-and-script-to-create-the-signing-service "Permanent link")

Creating the signing service

`[](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/en/latest/config/container_signing.html#__codelineno-3-1) django-admin add-signing-service container-default \ [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/en/latest/config/container_signing.html#__codelineno-3-2)   /var/lib/pulp/scripts/container_sign.sh \ [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/en/latest/config/container_signing.html#__codelineno-3-3)   ${FINGERPRINT} \ [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/en/latest/config/container_signing.html#__codelineno-3-4)   --class container:ManifestSigningService`

#### Configuring Galaxy to use the Signing Service[¶](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/en/latest/config/container_signing.html#configuring-galaxy-to-use-the-signing-service "Permanent link")

To tell galaxy which signing service to use you need to set it in the pulp settings.

Option 1:

/etc/pulp/settings.py

`[](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/en/latest/config/container_signing.html#__codelineno-4-1) GALAXY_CONTAINER_SIGNING_SERVICE = "unique-name"  #(1)`

1.  The name of the created signing service

Option 2:

Environment Variable

`[](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/en/latest/config/container_signing.html#__codelineno-5-1) export PULP_GALAXY_CONTAINER_SIGNING_SERVICE=unique-name #(1)`

1.  The name of the created signing service

#### Signing via API[¶](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/en/latest/config/container_signing.html#signing-via-api "Permanent link")

Galaxy calls Pulp Container API to trigger sign so refer to Pulp docs in case you need to use the API for signing [https://docs.pulpproject.org/pulp\_container/workflows/sign-images.html#sign-images-that-were-pushed-to-the-pulp-registry](https://docs.pulpproject.org/pulp_container/workflows/sign-images.html#sign-images-that-were-pushed-to-the-pulp-registry)

#### Signing via UI[¶](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/en/latest/config/container_signing.html#signing-via-ui "Permanent link")

On the UI under the **Execution Environment** menu there are buttons to trigger **sign** for a specific container image.

Info

To perform signing actions the user must be a superuser (admin) or need `modify_content_containerpushrepository` permissions provided also by the Roles execution `environment admin`, `execution environment publisher`, `execution environment namespace owner`, `execution environment collaborator`

Signature verification[¶](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/en/latest/config/container_signing.html#signature-verification "Permanent link")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### Obtaining the public key[¶](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/en/latest/config/container_signing.html#obtaining-the-public-key "Permanent link")

The public keys for the signing services on a Galaxy Server are exposed on the URL [https://FQDN/pulp/api/v3/signing-services/](https://fqdn/pulp/api/v3/signing-services/)
 and the respective UI page calles **Public Keys**

If the signature comes from a remote server then its public key must be found on the remote directly.

### Verification during installation[¶](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/en/latest/config/container_signing.html#verification-during-installation "Permanent link")

In order to verify a container image signature the client (podman, docker) needs to be configured with the **policy** file as described in [https://github.com/containers/image/blob/main/docs/containers-policy.json.5.md](https://github.com/containers/image/blob/main/docs/containers-policy.json.5.md)

Example:

`[](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/en/latest/config/container_signing.html#__codelineno-6-1) cat  /etc/containers/policy.json [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/en/latest/config/container_signing.html#__codelineno-6-2) { [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/en/latest/config/container_signing.html#__codelineno-6-3)   "default": [{"type": "reject"}], [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/en/latest/config/container_signing.html#__codelineno-6-4)   "transports": { [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/en/latest/config/container_signing.html#__codelineno-6-5)     "docker": { [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/en/latest/config/container_signing.html#__codelineno-6-6)        "fluffy.example.com": [ [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/en/latest/config/container_signing.html#__codelineno-6-7)         { [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/en/latest/config/container_signing.html#__codelineno-6-8)           "type": "signedBy", [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/en/latest/config/container_signing.html#__codelineno-6-9)           "keyType": "GPGKeys", [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/en/latest/config/container_signing.html#__codelineno-6-10)           "keyPath": "/path-to-pupsik-key.gpg" [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/en/latest/config/container_signing.html#__codelineno-6-11)         } [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/en/latest/config/container_signing.html#__codelineno-6-12)       ] [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/en/latest/config/container_signing.html#__codelineno-6-13)     }, [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/en/latest/config/container_signing.html#__codelineno-6-14)     "containers-storage": { [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/en/latest/config/container_signing.html#__codelineno-6-15)     "": [{"type": "insecureAcceptAnything"}] /* Allow copy operations on any images stored in containers storage (e.g. podman push) */ [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/en/latest/config/container_signing.html#__codelineno-6-16)     } [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/en/latest/config/container_signing.html#__codelineno-6-17)   } [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/en/latest/config/container_signing.html#__codelineno-6-18) }`

The execution of client command must be like described on [https://docs.pulpproject.org/pulp\_container/workflows/verify-images.html#verify-images-pushed-into-pulp-container-registry](https://docs.pulpproject.org/pulp_container/workflows/verify-images.html#verify-images-pushed-into-pulp-container-registry)

Client verifying container image signature

`[](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/en/latest/config/container_signing.html#__codelineno-7-1) podman pull fluffy.example.com/myrepo/test-image:foo [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/en/latest/config/container_signing.html#__codelineno-7-2) Trying to pull fluffy.example.com/myrepo/test-image:foo... [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/en/latest/config/container_signing.html#__codelineno-7-3) Getting image source signatures [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/en/latest/config/container_signing.html#__codelineno-7-4) Checking if image destination supports signatures [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/en/latest/config/container_signing.html#__codelineno-7-5) Copying blob 58147e24f776 skipped: already exists [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/en/latest/config/container_signing.html#__codelineno-7-6) Copying config 829374d342 done [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/en/latest/config/container_signing.html#__codelineno-7-7) Writing manifest to image destination [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/en/latest/config/container_signing.html#__codelineno-7-8) Storing signatures [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/en/latest/config/container_signing.html#__codelineno-7-9) 829374d342ae65a12f3a95911bc04a001894349f70783fda841b1a784008727d`

Back to top

# Ansible AWX Operator Documentation

 

[Skip to content](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/awx-operator/en/latest/#awx-operator)

[](https://github.com/ansible/awx-operator/blob/devel/docs/index.md "Edit this page")

AWX Operator[¶](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/awx-operator/en/latest/#awx-operator "Permanent link")

=========================================================================================================

[![License](https://img.shields.io/badge/License-Apache%202.0-blue.svg)](https://opensource.org/licenses/Apache-2.0)
 [![Build Status](https://github.com/ansible/awx-operator/workflows/CI/badge.svg?event=push)](https://github.com/ansible/awx-operator/actions)
 [![Code of Conduct](https://img.shields.io/badge/code%20of%20conduct-Ansible-yellow.svg)](https://docs.ansible.com/ansible/latest/community/code_of_conduct.html)

An [Ansible AWX](https://github.com/ansible/awx)
 operator for Kubernetes built with [Operator SDK](https://github.com/operator-framework/operator-sdk)
 and Ansible.

The AWX Operator is meant to be deployed in your Kubernetes cluster(s) and can be used to install and manage the lifecycle of an AWX instance in the same namespace.

Documentation[¶](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/awx-operator/en/latest/#documentation "Permanent link")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The AWX Operator documentation is available at [https://ansible.readthedocs.io/projects/awx-operator/](https://ansible.readthedocs.io/projects/awx-operator/)

> Helm chart documentation is available at [https://ansible-community.github.io/awx-operator-helm/](https://ansible-community.github.io/awx-operator-helm/)

Contributing[¶](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/awx-operator/en/latest/#contributing "Permanent link")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Please visit [our contributing guidelines](https://github.com/ansible/awx-operator/blob/devel/CONTRIBUTING.md)
.

For docs changes, create PRs on the appropriate files in the `/docs` folder.

The development environment consists of running an [`up.sh`](https://github.com/ansible/awx-operator/blob/devel/up.sh)
 and a [`down.sh`](https://github.com/ansible/awx-operator/blob/devel/down.sh)
 script, which applies or deletes yaml on the Openshift or K8s cluster you are connected to. See the [development.md](https://github.com/ansible/awx-operator/blob/devel/docs/development.md)
 for information on how to deploy and test changes from your branch.

Author[¶](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/awx-operator/en/latest/#author "Permanent link")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

This operator was originally built in 2019 by [Jeff Geerling](https://www.jeffgeerling.com/)
 and is now maintained by the Ansible Team

Code of Conduct[¶](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/awx-operator/en/latest/#code-of-conduct "Permanent link")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

We ask all of our community members and contributors to adhere to the [Ansible code of conduct](http://docs.ansible.com/ansible/latest/community/code_of_conduct.html)
. If you have questions or need assistance, please reach out to our community team at [codeofconduct@ansible.com](mailto:codeofconduct@ansible.com)

Get Involved[¶](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/awx-operator/en/latest/#get-involved "Permanent link")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

We welcome your feedback, questions and ideas. Here's how to reach the community.

### Forum[¶](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/awx-operator/en/latest/#forum "Permanent link")

Join the [Ansible Forum](https://forum.ansible.com/)
 as a single starting point and our default communication platform for questions and help, development discussions, events, and much more. [Register](https://forum.ansible.com/signup?)
 to join the community. Search by categories and tags to find interesting topics or start a new one; subscribe only to topics you need!

*   [Get Help](https://forum.ansible.com/c/help/6)
    : get help or help others. Please add appropriate tags if you start new discussions, for example `awx-operator` and `documentation`.
*   [Posts tagged with 'awx-operator'](https://forum.ansible.com/tag/awx-operator)
    : subscribe to participate in project-related conversations.
*   [Bullhorn newsletter](https://docs.ansible.com/ansible/devel/community/communication.html#the-bullhorn)
     used to announce releases and important changes.
*   [Social Spaces](https://forum.ansible.com/c/chat/4)
    : gather and interact with fellow enthusiasts.
*   [News & Announcements](https://forum.ansible.com/c/news/5)
    : track project-wide announcements including social events.

For more information on the forum navigation, see [Navigating the Ansible forum](https://forum.ansible.com/t/navigating-the-ansible-forum-tags-categories-and-concepts/39)
 post.

### Matrix[¶](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/awx-operator/en/latest/#matrix "Permanent link")

For real-time interactions, conversations in the community happen over the Matrix protocol in the following channels:

*   [#awx:ansible.com](https://matrix.to/#/#awx:ansible.com)
    : AWX and AWX-Operator project-related discussions.
*   [#docs:ansible.im](https://matrix.to/#/#docs:ansible.im)
    : Ansible, AWX and AWX-Operator documentation-related discussions.

For more information, see the community-hosted [Matrix FAQ](https://hackmd.io/@ansible-community/community-matrix-faq)
.

# Ansible Onboarding toolkit documentation

 

Home
====

Welcome to the Ansible project onboarding toolkit!

The purpose of this toolkit is to provide you with guidance as you consider adding your project to the Ansible ecosystem of projects.

This toolkit provides guidelines on the project GitHub repository setup as well as the type of documentation your project should include. It also provides a GitHub [repository template](https://github.com/ansible-community/project-template)
 that you can use to create a repository in the GitHub [ansible-community](https://github.com/ansible-community)
 organization.

These guidelines are specific to projects being added to the official community Ansible ecosystem projects in the [ansible-community](https://github.com/ansible-community)
 organization. This does not apply to Ansible collections or other projects not part of the ecosystem.

---

# home - Ansible Pytest Documentation

 

[Skip to content](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/pytest-ansible/#ansible-pytest-documentation)

[](https://github.com/ansible/pytest-ansible/blob/main/docs/index.md "Edit this page")

Ansible Pytest Documentation[¶](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/pytest-ansible/#ansible-pytest-documentation "Permanent link")

=================================================================================================================================

About Ansible Pytest[¶](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/pytest-ansible/#about-ansible-pytest "Permanent link")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The `pytest-ansible` plugin is designed to provide seamless integration between `pytest` and `Ansible`, allowing you to efficiently run and test Ansible-related tasks and scenarios within your pytest test suite. This plugin enhances the testing workflow by offering three distinct pieces of functionality:

1.  **Unit Testing for Ansible Collections**: This feature aids in running unit tests for `Ansible collections` using `pytest`. It allows you to validate the behavior of your Ansible `modules` and `roles` in isolation, ensuring that each component functions as expected.
    
2.  **Molecule Scenario Integration**: The plugin assists in running Molecule `scenarios` using `pytest`. This integration streamlines the testing of Ansible roles and playbooks across different environments, making it easier to identify and fix issues across diverse setups.
    
3.  **Ansible Integration for Pytest Tests**: With this functionality, you can seamlessly use `Ansible` from within your `pytest` tests. This opens up possibilities to interact with Ansible components and perform tasks like provisioning resources, testing configurations, and more, all while leveraging the power and flexibility of pytest.

---

# Installing - Ansible Pytest Documentation

 

[Skip to content](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/pytest-ansible/installing/#installing)

[](https://github.com/ansible/pytest-ansible/blob/main/docs/installing.md "Edit this page")

Installing[¶](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/pytest-ansible/installing/#installing "Permanent link")

========================================================================================================

Requirements[¶](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/pytest-ansible/installing/#requirements "Permanent link")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   Ansible 2.9 or newer
*   Pytest

Installing the latest version[¶](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/pytest-ansible/installing/#installing-the-latest-version "Permanent link")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

You can install the most recent version of pytest-ansible with the \[pip\] Python package manager.

`[](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/pytest-ansible/installing/#__codelineno-0-1) pip install pytest-ansible`

Install this plugin using `pip`:

`[](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/pytest-ansible/installing/#__codelineno-1-1) pip install pytest-ansible`

# Development - Galaxy NG

 

[Skip to content](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/en/latest/community/devstack.html#community-development-stack)

[](https://github.com/ansible/galaxy_ng/edit/main/docs/community/devstack.md "Edit this page")
[](https://github.com/ansible/galaxy_ng/raw/main/docs/community/devstack.md "View source of this page")

Community Development Stack[¶](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/en/latest/community/devstack.html#community-development-stack "Permanent link")

===========================================================================================================================================================

The architecture[¶](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/en/latest/community/devstack.html#the-architecture "Permanent link")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

As noted in the overview, GalaxyNG is considered a [pulp plugin](https://docs.pulpproject.org/pulpcore/plugins/index.html)
. As opposed to more vanilla django application, pulp plugins are extensions to the pulpcore django application. Most of the plugin application code is extending or making use of pulp's underlying primitives such as "distributions", "repositories" and "content".

Governance of the various projects has increasingly leaned towards writing less code in the galaxy\_ng repository and more in the pulp\_ansible repository. The code in the pulp\_ansible repository is sufficiently generic to be usable for not only GalaxyNG but also as components in the Red Hat Satellite product. GalaxyNG is slowly turning into more of an "integration project" than a project with unique features. This pattern of where to write code has slightly diverged in the case of the api/v1 codebase because of differences in pulp\_ansible's implementation that are incompatible with many of the roles indexed by [galaxy](https://galaxy.ansible.com/)
.

As a hopeful contributor, you may need to first figure out where to write code for a new feature or a bugfix. General guidance is to start any new enhancements for api/v3 in the pulp\_ansible project. Anything for api/v1 is in the galaxy\_ng project. If you aren't sure or can't figure out where the code should be, please reach out on the [Ansible forum](https://docs.ansible.com/ansible/devel/community/communication.html)
 and we'll help guide you.

### Services[¶](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/en/latest/community/devstack.html#services "Permanent link")

*   api server
*   worker(s)
*   content app
*   postgres

### Related Projects[¶](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/en/latest/community/devstack.html#related-projects "Permanent link")

*   [https://github.com/ansible/galaxy\_ng](https://github.com/ansible/galaxy_ng)
    
*   [https://github.com/ansible/ansible-hub-ui](https://github.com/ansible/ansible-hub-ui)
    
*   [https://github.com/pulp/pulp\_ansible](https://github.com/pulp/pulp_ansible)
    
*   [https://github.com/pulp/pulp\_container](https://github.com/pulp/pulp_container)
    
*   [https://github.com/pulp/pulpcore](https://github.com/pulp/pulpcore)
    

### api/v1[¶](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/en/latest/community/devstack.html#apiv1 "Permanent link")

Found in [https://github.com/ansible/galaxy\_ng/tree/main/galaxy\_ng/app/api/v1](https://github.com/ansible/galaxy_ng/tree/main/galaxy_ng/app/api/v1)

The v1 api was re-implemented in GalaxyNG from scratch as simple django models, views, serializers and filtersets. This api version allows for role imports and indexing from [github](https://github.com/)
 in the same conceptual way that roles were handled in [galaxy](https://galaxy.ansible.com/)
. Two models comprise most of how api/v1 works under the covers: "LegacyRole" and "LegacyNamespace".

Access control in api/v1 is controlled by a model we have termed a "Legacy Namespace". These differ from the namespaces found in api/v3 because of naming restrictions there which aren't suitable for many of the usernames that have been created on [galaxy](https://galaxy.ansible.com/)
. Each legacy namespace has a list of "owners", or github usernames that have control over the namespace to add/remove owners, upload new roles, delete roles, etc. The social auth handler will automatically create a legacy namespace and set the owner for logged in users if one does not already exist.

### api/v3[¶](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/en/latest/community/devstack.html#apiv3 "Permanent link")

Found in [https://github.com/ansible/galaxy\_ng/tree/main/galaxy\_ng/app/api/v3](https://github.com/ansible/galaxy_ng/tree/main/galaxy_ng/app/api/v3)

The v3 api is 100% focused on collections and collection namespaces. As time progresses, the v3 api becomes more and more a redirect to endpoints provided by pulp\_ansible.

Access control over collections is controlled by the v3 collection namespaces and pulp object level RBAC. Users are added to a group and the group is bound to the namespace with a permission. For the community instances, the group and permission bindings are handled automatically by the social auth handler.

### Docker Compose Env[¶](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/en/latest/community/devstack.html#docker-compose-env "Permanent link")

To get started, it's recommended to a create a new top level directory to store your various checkouts.

``[](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/en/latest/community/devstack.html#__codelineno-0-1) /src [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/en/latest/community/devstack.html#__codelineno-0-2) `- galaxy_ng [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/en/latest/community/devstack.html#__codelineno-0-3) `- ansible-hub-ui``

Clone the projects into the src dir.

`[](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/en/latest/community/devstack.html#__codelineno-1-1) cd src [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/en/latest/community/devstack.html#__codelineno-1-2) git clone https://github.com/ansible/galaxy_ng [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/en/latest/community/devstack.html#__codelineno-1-3) git clone https://github.com/ansible/ansible-hub-ui`

Start the stack build and spinup

`[](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/en/latest/community/devstack.html#__codelineno-2-1) cd src/galaxy_ng [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/en/latest/community/devstack.html#__codelineno-2-2) make compose/community`

You can now reach the api at [http://localhost:5001](http://localhost:5001/)
. The makefile targets created a couple test users, namely admin:admin for the primary superuser. This is purely an API stack with no UI. That makes it difficult to test and use github users, so see the next section about adding the UI.

### React.js UI[¶](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/en/latest/community/devstack.html#reactjs-ui "Permanent link")

To connect to the UI, the default address is [http://localhost:8002](http://localhost:8002/)
. If social auth is enabled, you'll be forced to auth via Github if clicking on the "login" link at the top right of the page. The presence of `SOCIAL_AUTH_GITHUB_KEY` & `SOCIAL_AUTH_GITHUB_SECRET` in the backend configuration triggers dynaconf to set a feature flag for external auth that the UI reads and alters the login link accordingly. If you want to bypass github and use a local django user, go directly to the [http://localhost:8002/ui/login](http://localhost:8002/ui/login)
 url and login that way.

#### starting directly[¶](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/en/latest/community/devstack.html#starting-directly "Permanent link")

For development work on the UI, it's easiest to launch the webpack dev server outside the compose stack. The npm start scripts and configuration will have it redirect api calls to [http://localhost:5001](http://localhost:5001/)
 by default, so no hacking is necessary to make it work locally.

1.  Install [NVM](https://github.com/nvm-sh/nvm)
     to easily manage node.js versions.
2.  Use NVM to install and set the version to 18: `nvm install 18`,
3.  Clone the [ansible-hub-ui](https://github.com/ansible/ansible-hub-ui)
     repo to the desired location.
4.  From the checkout, run `npm install`.
5.  From the checkout, run `npm run start-community`.

### Configuration[¶](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/en/latest/community/devstack.html#configuration "Permanent link")

The galaxy\_ng project is a highly configurable application, but only at startup time. Almost all of the behavior of the application is influenced by environment variables and static settings.py files that are read in by [django](https://www.djangoproject.com/)
 and [dynaconf](https://www.dynaconf.com/)
. None of these settings live in the database currently, so they can only be declared before the application starts. If you change anything after starting the stack, it is recommeneded to completely destroy the stack, wipe all your docker images and spin up from scatch.

The community specific settings are found in `galaxy_ng/profiles/community/pulp_config.env`

1.  Comment out the `SOCIAL_AUTH_GITHUB_BASE_URL` line
2.  Add `PULP_SOCIAL_AUTH_GITHUB_KEY=<value>`
3.  Add `PULP_SOCIAL_AUTH_GITHUB_SECRET=<value>`

The github key and secret are provided by configuring an oauth account on github.com under your user's developer settings. The oauth application config needs to have a valid and resolvable homepage url and the callback url should be the same host with "/complete/github/" as the path. For local testing, you can use [http://localhost:8002](http://localhost:8002/)
 as base url.

Testing[¶](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/en/latest/community/devstack.html#testing "Permanent link")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The GalaxyNG project is a "test driven development" (TDD) project. Our interpretation of TDD is that -every- PR to the main branch -must- have some sort of test or test changes. It can be unit, functional or integration, but something must be written. It doesn't matter if the PR is a bugfix or a feature, tests must be written. This is how we avoid regressions and keep track of how new code is meant to work inside a very complex and spread out architecture with many different configurable behaviors.

### Unit[¶](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/en/latest/community/devstack.html#unit "Permanent link")

More focus has been spent on the integration tests, but there are a few unit tests in the project. The compose stack must be running to launch unit tests, as they are using django and postgres with a temporary database to do real SQL calls that are incompatible with sqlite.

To launch units, execute `make docker/test/unit`. Specific tests can be selected by running `TEST=xyz make docker/test/unit`.

### Functional[¶](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/en/latest/community/devstack.html#functional "Permanent link")

Pulp has a testing concept known as [functional tests](https://docs.pulpproject.org/pulpcore/en/master/nightly/contributing/tests.html#functional-tests)
. These are mostly using a suite of fixtures, auto-generated api clients and strict style to do integration testing. The environment needed to run functional tests is somewhat complicated to setup, so the GalaxyNG project instead writes integration tests. However, if you end up writing patches for any of the repositories in [https://github.com/pulp](https://github.com/pulp)
, you will be required to write functional tests.

### Integration[¶](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/en/latest/community/devstack.html#integration "Permanent link")

The GalaxyNG project backs the Red Hat product named "Automation HUB". As it is a product, it has a formal QE team testing release features with their own private internal frameworks built around pytest. We decided to "shift left" on integration testing and put it on developers to write integration tests along with their pullrequests (TDD). The initial batch of integration tests came from forking the internal tests and pulling out elements of the testing framework that were either too complicated or reliant on systems behind the corporate firewall. Since that time, the tests have grown substantially and comprise a much larger level of coverage. The integration tests aim to be as simple as possible to run and "framework light" in the sense that you really only need pytest, python-requests and ansible-core. Most integration tests are instantiating a REST api client that then talks to the api and asserts certain behaviors such as return codes, response types/shape and errors.
