# Ansible-Native Configuration - Ansible Molecule

 

[Skip to content](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#ansible-native-configuration)

[](https://github.com/ansible/molecule/blob/main/docs/ansible-native.md "Edit this page")
[](https://github.com/ansible/molecule/raw/main/docs/ansible-native.md "View source of this page")

Ansible-Native Configuration[¶](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#ansible-native-configuration "Permanent link")

==========================================================================================================================================

This document provides technical reference for the ansible-native configuration approach. The ansible-native approach leverages standard Ansible inventory, collections, and playbooks for testing resource management.

Configuration Structure[¶](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#configuration-structure "Permanent link")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Ansible-native molecule configurations use the following top-level sections:

`[](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-0-1) --- [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-0-2) ansible: [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-0-3)   executor: [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-0-4)     backend: ansible-playbook [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-0-5)     args: [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-0-6)       ansible_playbook: [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-0-7)         - --inventory=inventory/ [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-0-8)   env: [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-0-9)     ANSIBLE_FORCE_COLOR: true [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-0-10)   cfg: [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-0-11)     defaults: [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-0-12)       host_key_checking: false [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-0-13)   playbooks: [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-0-14)     create: create.yml [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-0-15)     destroy: destroy.yml [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-0-16)     converge: converge.yml [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-0-17)     verify: verify.yml [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-0-18) [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-0-19) scenario: [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-0-20)   test_sequence: [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-0-21)     - create [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-0-22)     - converge [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-0-23)     - verify [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-0-24)     - destroy [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-0-25) [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-0-26) shared_state: true [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-0-27) [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-0-28) verifier: [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-0-29)   name: ansible`

Ansible Configuration Section[¶](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#ansible-configuration-section "Permanent link")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The `ansible` section contains all Ansible-specific configuration.

### Executor Configuration[¶](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#executor-configuration "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-1-1) ansible: [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-1-2)   executor: [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-1-3)     backend: ansible-playbook [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-1-4)     args: [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-1-5)       ansible_playbook: [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-1-6)         - --inventory=inventory/ [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-1-7)         - --extra-vars=environment=test [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-1-8)       ansible_navigator: [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-1-9)         - --execution-environment-image=custom:latest [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-1-10)         - --inventory=inventory/`

*   `backend`: Execution backend (ansible-playbook or ansible-navigator)
*   `args`: Command-line arguments passed to the executor

### Executor Environment Variables[¶](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#executor-environment-variables "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-2-1) ansible: [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-2-2)   env: [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-2-3)     ANSIBLE_INVENTORY: inventory/ [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-2-4)     ANSIBLE_FORCE_COLOR: true [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-2-5)     ANSIBLE_STDOUT_CALLBACK: yaml [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-2-6)     ANSIBLE_HOST_KEY_CHECKING: false [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-2-7)     ANSIBLE_COLLECTIONS_PATH: collections/`

Environment variables are passed to all ansible executions. `ANSIBLE_INVENTORY` specifies the inventory source.

### Ansible Configuration[¶](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#ansible-configuration "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-3-1) ansible: [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-3-2)   cfg: [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-3-3)     defaults: [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-3-4)       inventory: inventory/ [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-3-5)       host_key_checking: false [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-3-6)       fact_caching: memory [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-3-7)       gathering: smart [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-3-8)       timeout: 30 [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-3-9)     ssh_connection: [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-3-10)       ssh_args: -o ControlMaster=auto -o ControlPersist=60s [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-3-11)       pipelining: true`

Configuration options merged into ansible.cfg for the scenario. The `inventory` setting under defaults specifies the inventory source.

### Playbook Paths[¶](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#playbook-paths "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-4-1) ansible: [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-4-2)   playbooks: [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-4-3)     create: create.yml [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-4-4)     destroy: destroy.yml [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-4-5)     converge: converge.yml [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-4-6)     prepare: prepare.yml [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-4-7)     verify: verify.yml [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-4-8)     cleanup: cleanup.yml [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-4-9)     side_effect: side_effect.yml`

Playbook paths are relative to the scenario directory.

Native Inventory Usage[¶](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#native-inventory-usage "Permanent link")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Ansible-native approach uses standard Ansible inventory sources.

### Static YAML Inventory[¶](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#static-yaml-inventory "Permanent link")

#### Host-Based Testing[¶](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#host-based-testing "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-5-1) # inventory/hosts.yml [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-5-2) all: [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-5-3)   children: [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-5-4)     web_servers: [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-5-5)       hosts: [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-5-6)         web-test-01: [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-5-7)           ansible_host: 192.168.1.10 [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-5-8)           ansible_user: testuser [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-5-9)           ansible_ssh_private_key_file: ~/.ssh/test_key [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-5-10)         web-test-02: [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-5-11)           ansible_host: 192.168.1.11 [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-5-12)           ansible_user: testuser [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-5-13)           ansible_ssh_private_key_file: ~/.ssh/test_key [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-5-14)       vars: [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-5-15)         nginx_port: 8080 [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-5-16)         ssl_enabled: false [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-5-17)     database_servers: [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-5-18)       hosts: [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-5-19)         db-test-01: [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-5-20)           ansible_host: 192.168.1.20 [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-5-21)           ansible_user: dbadmin [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-5-22)           ansible_ssh_private_key_file: ~/.ssh/test_key [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-5-23)       vars: [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-5-24)         postgresql_version: 13 [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-5-25)         backup_enabled: false`

#### API Endpoint Testing[¶](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#api-endpoint-testing "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-6-1) # inventory/api.yml [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-6-2) all: [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-6-3)   children: [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-6-4)     api_endpoints: [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-6-5)       hosts: [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-6-6)         auth-api: [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-6-7)           endpoint_url: https://auth-test.company.com/api/v1 [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-6-8)           health_check_path: /health [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-6-9)           timeout: 30 [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-6-10)         payment-api: [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-6-11)           endpoint_url: https://payment-test.company.com/api/v2 [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-6-12)           health_check_path: /status [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-6-13)           timeout: 45 [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-6-14)         user-api: [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-6-15)           endpoint_url: https://user-test.company.com/api/v1 [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-6-16)           health_check_path: /ping [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-6-17)           timeout: 20 [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-6-18)       vars: [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-6-19)         api_key: "{{ vault_test_api_key }}" [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-6-20)         verify_ssl: true [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-6-21)         testing_environment: integration`

#### Database Testing[¶](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#database-testing "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-7-1) # inventory/database.yml [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-7-2) all: [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-7-3)   children: [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-7-4)     database_instances: [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-7-5)       hosts: [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-7-6)         postgres-test: [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-7-7)           db_host: postgres-test.company.com [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-7-8)           db_port: 5432 [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-7-9)           db_name: test_database [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-7-10)           db_user: test_user [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-7-11)           db_password: "{{ vault_postgres_test_password }}" [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-7-12)           connection_timeout: 30 [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-7-13)         mysql-test: [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-7-14)           db_host: mysql-test.company.com [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-7-15)           db_port: 3306 [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-7-16)           db_name: test_schema [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-7-17)           db_user: test_user [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-7-18)           db_password: "{{ vault_mysql_test_password }}" [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-7-19)           connection_timeout: 15 [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-7-20)         redis-test: [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-7-21)           db_host: redis-test.company.com [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-7-22)           db_port: 6379 [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-7-23)           db_password: "{{ vault_redis_test_password }}" [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-7-24)           connection_timeout: 10 [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-7-25)       vars: [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-7-26)         testing_mode: true [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-7-27)         max_connections: 10`

### Dynamic Inventory Plugin[¶](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#dynamic-inventory-plugin "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-8-1) # inventory/dynamic.yml [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-8-2) plugin: amazon.aws.aws_ec2 [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-8-3) regions: [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-8-4)   - us-east-1 [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-8-5) filters: [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-8-6)   "tag:Environment": "test" # Only instances with tag Environment=test [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-8-7) keyed_groups: [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-8-8)   - key: tags.Name [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-8-9)     prefix: tag`

### Constructed Inventory[¶](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#constructed-inventory "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-9-1) # inventory/constructed.yml [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-9-2) plugin: constructed [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-9-3) strict: false [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-9-4) compose: [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-9-5)   ansible_host: ansible_host | default(inventory_hostname) [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-9-6)   component_role: group_names | select('match', '^role_') | first | default('undefined') [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-9-7) groups: [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-9-8)   web_servers: "'web' in component_type" [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-9-9)   database_servers: "'database' in component_type" [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-9-10)   testing: "environment == 'test'"`

### Enterprise Inventory Integration[¶](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#enterprise-inventory-integration "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-10-1) # inventory/enterprise.yml [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-10-2) all: [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-10-3)   vars: [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-10-4)     ansible_user: "{{ vault_ssh_user }}" [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-10-5)     ansible_ssh_private_key_file: "{{ vault_ssh_key_path }}" Shared State Configuration[¶](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#shared-state-configuration "Permanent link")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Shared state enables scenarios to share ephemeral state and testing resources.

### Configuration[¶](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#configuration "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-11-1) shared_state: true`

When enabled:

*   All scenarios share the same ephemeral state directory
*   Default scenario manages testing resource lifecycle
*   Component scenarios access shared resources
*   State persists between scenario executions

### Resource Lifecycle Management[¶](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#resource-lifecycle-management "Permanent link")

**Default scenario** (testing resource management):

`[](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-12-1) scenario: [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-12-2)   test_sequence: [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-12-3)     - create [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-12-4)     - destroy`

**Component scenarios** (testing only):

`[](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-13-1) scenario: [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-13-2)   test_sequence: [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-13-3)     - prepare [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-13-4)     - converge [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-13-5)     - verify [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-13-6)     - cleanup`

### Execution Flow[¶](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#execution-flow "Permanent link")

With `shared_state: true`:

1.  Default scenario creates shared testing resources
2.  Component scenarios execute tests against shared resources
3.  Default scenario destroys shared testing resources

Collection-Based Resource Management[¶](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#collection-based-resource-management "Permanent link")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The ansible-native approach leverages ansible collections for testing resource management.

### Container Testing Resources[¶](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#container-testing-resources "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-14-1) # create.yml [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-14-2) --- [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-14-3) - name: Create testing containers [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-14-4)   hosts: localhost [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-14-5)   gather_facts: false [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-14-6)   tasks: [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-14-7)     - name: Create container network [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-14-8)       containers.podman.podman_network: [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-14-9)         name: molecule-test [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-14-10)         state: present [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-14-11) [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-14-12)     - name: Create test containers [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-14-13)       containers.podman.podman_container: [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-14-14)         name: "{{ item }}" [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-14-15)         image: "{{ hostvars[item].image }}" [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-14-16)         state: started [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-14-17)         networks: [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-14-18)           - molecule-test [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-14-19)         published_ports: [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-14-20)           - "{{ hostvars[item].port | default('0') }}:{{ hostvars[item].internal_port | default('80') }}" [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-14-21)       loop: "{{ groups['test_resources'] }}" [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-14-22) [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-14-23)     - name: Wait for containers [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-14-24)       ansible.builtin.wait_for: [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-14-25)         port: "{{ hostvars[item].internal_port | default('80') }}" [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-14-26)         host: "{{ hostvars[item].ansible_host }}" [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-14-27)         delay: 5 [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-14-28)       loop: "{{ groups['test_resources'] }}"`

### Cloud Testing Resources[¶](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#cloud-testing-resources "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-15-1) # create.yml [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-15-2) --- [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-15-3) - name: Create AWS testing resources [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-15-4)   hosts: localhost [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-15-5)   gather_facts: false [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-15-6)   tasks: [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-15-7)     - name: Create VPC [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-15-8)       amazon.aws.ec2_vpc_net: [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-15-9)         name: molecule-test-vpc [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-15-10)         cidr_block: 10.0.0.0/16 [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-15-11)         region: "{{ aws_region }}" [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-15-12)         state: present [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-15-13)       register: vpc [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-15-14) [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-15-15)     - name: Create security group [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-15-16)       amazon.aws.ec2_security_group: [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-15-17)         name: molecule-test-sg [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-15-18)         description: Molecule testing security group [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-15-19)         vpc_id: "{{ vpc.vpc.id }}" [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-15-20)         region: "{{ aws_region }}" [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-15-21)         rules: [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-15-22)           - proto: tcp [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-15-23)             ports: [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-15-24)               - 22 [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-15-25)               - 80 [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-15-26)             cidr_ip: 0.0.0.0/0 [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-15-27)         state: present [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-15-28)       register: sg [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-15-29) [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-15-30)     - name: Launch instances [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-15-31)       amazon.aws.ec2_instance: [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-15-32)         name: "{{ item }}" [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-15-33)         image_id: "{{ hostvars[item].ami_id }}" [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-15-34)         instance_type: "{{ hostvars[item].instance_type }}" [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-15-35)         vpc_subnet_id: "{{ vpc.vpc.id }}" [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-15-36)         security_group: "{{ sg.group_id }}" [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-15-37)         region: "{{ aws_region }}" [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-15-38)         state: started [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-15-39)         wait: true [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-15-40)       loop: "{{ groups['test_resources'] }}"`

### Collection Dependencies[¶](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#collection-dependencies "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-16-1) # requirements.yml [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-16-2) --- [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-16-3) collections: [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-16-4)   - name: containers.podman [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-16-5)     version: ">=1.9.0" [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-16-6)   - name: amazon.aws [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-16-7)     version: ">=5.0.0" [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-16-8)   - name: community.general [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-16-9)     version: ">=6.0.0"`

Scenario Types[¶](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#scenario-types "Permanent link")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### Collection Component Testing[¶](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#collection-component-testing "Permanent link")

Test individual collection components (roles, modules, plugins):

`[](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-17-1) # molecule.yml [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-17-2) --- [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-17-3) ansible: [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-17-4)   executor: [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-17-5)     args: [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-17-6)       ansible_playbook: [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-17-7)         - --inventory=inventory/ [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-17-8)   playbooks: [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-17-9)     converge: converge.yml [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-17-10)     verify: verify.yml [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-17-11) [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-17-12) scenario: [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-17-13)   test_sequence: [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-17-14)     - converge [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-17-15)     - verify [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-17-16)     - idempotence`

`[](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-18-1) # converge.yml [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-18-2) --- [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-18-3) - name: Test collection role [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-18-4)   hosts: test_resources [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-18-5)   tasks: [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-18-6)     - name: Include collection role [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-18-7)       ansible.builtin.include_role: [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-18-8)         name: my_namespace.my_collection.my_role [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-18-9)       vars: [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-18-10)         role_config: test_value`

### Playbook Testing[¶](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#playbook-testing "Permanent link")

Test complete playbooks:

`[](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-19-1) # converge.yml [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-19-2) --- [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-19-3) - name: Execute test playbook [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-19-4)   ansible.builtin.import_playbook: "{{ molecule_project_directory }}/playbooks/site.yml"`

### Multi-Scenario Collection Testing[¶](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#multi-scenario-collection-testing "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-20-1) # Base config.yml [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-20-2) --- [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-20-3) ansible: [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-20-4)   executor: [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-20-5)     args: [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-20-6)       ansible_playbook: [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-20-7)         - --inventory={{ molecule_scenario_directory }}/../inventory/ [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-20-8) [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-20-9) scenario: [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-20-10)   test_sequence: [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-20-11)     - converge [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-20-12)     - verify [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-20-13) [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-20-14) shared_state: true`

**Default scenario** manages testing resources:

`[](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-21-1) # default/molecule.yml [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-21-2) --- [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-21-3) scenario: [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-21-4)   test_sequence: [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-21-5)     - create [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-21-6)     - destroy`

**Component scenarios** inherit base configuration and test specific components.

### Integration Testing Patterns[¶](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#integration-testing-patterns "Permanent link")

Multi-component testing with shared resources:

`[](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-22-1) # verify.yml [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-22-2) --- [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-22-3) - name: Integration tests [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-22-4)   hosts: web_servers [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-22-5)   tasks: [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-22-6)     - name: Test web service responds [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-22-7)       ansible.builtin.uri: [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-22-8)         url: "http://{{ ansible_host }}:80/health" [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-22-9)         method: GET [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-22-10)         status_code: 200 [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-22-11) [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-22-12)     - name: Test database connectivity [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-22-13)       ansible.builtin.wait_for: [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-22-14)         host: "{{ hostvars[groups['database_servers'][0]].ansible_host }}" [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-22-15)         port: 5432 [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-22-16)         timeout: 10 [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-22-17) [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-22-18) - name: Cross-component tests [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-22-19)   hosts: test_resources [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-22-20)   tasks: [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-22-21)     - name: Verify service mesh communication [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-22-22)       ansible.builtin.uri: [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-22-23)         url: "http://{{ ansible_host }}/api/status" [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-22-24)         return_content: true [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-22-25)       register: api_response [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-22-26) [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-22-27)     - name: Assert response format [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-22-28)       ansible.builtin.assert: [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-22-29)         that: [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-22-30)           - api_response.json.status == "healthy" [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-22-31)           - api_response.json.version is defined`

Verifier Configuration[¶](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#verifier-configuration "Permanent link")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The ansible verifier executes verification playbooks using the same inventory and configuration as other testing phases.

### Basic Configuration[¶](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#basic-configuration "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-23-1) verifier: [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-23-2)   name: ansible [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-23-3)   enabled: true`

### Environment Variables[¶](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#environment-variables "Permanent link")

Environment variables for verification should be configured in the `ansible` section:

`[](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-24-1) ansible: [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-24-2)   env: [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-24-3)     PYTHONPATH: /custom/test/path [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-24-4)     TEST_DATABASE_URL: postgresql://test:test@localhost/test [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-24-5)     ANSIBLE_FORCE_COLOR: true`

Verification playbooks have access to all molecule environment variables and scenario configuration.

Complete Example[¶](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#complete-example "Permanent link")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

`[](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-25-1) --- [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-25-2) ansible: [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-25-3)   executor: [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-25-4)     backend: ansible-playbook [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-25-5)     args: [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-25-6)       ansible_playbook: [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-25-7)         - --inventory=inventory/ [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-25-8)         - --extra-vars=test_mode=true [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-25-9)   env: [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-25-10)     ANSIBLE_FORCE_COLOR: true [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-25-11)     ANSIBLE_HOST_KEY_CHECKING: false [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-25-12)   cfg: [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-25-13)     defaults: [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-25-14)       fact_caching: memory [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-25-15)       gathering: smart [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-25-16)   playbooks: [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-25-17)     create: create.yml [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-25-18)     destroy: destroy.yml [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-25-19)     converge: converge.yml [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-25-20)     verify: verify.yml [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-25-21) [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-25-22) scenario: [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-25-23)   test_sequence: [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-25-24)     - create [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-25-25)     - converge [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-25-26)     - verify [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-25-27)     - destroy [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-25-28) [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-25-29) shared_state: true [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-25-30) [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-25-31) verifier: [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#__codelineno-25-32)   name: ansible`

This configuration defines ansible-native testing with:

*   Native inventory sources in `inventory/` directory
*   Collection-based testing resource management in create/destroy playbooks
*   Shared state for multi-scenario testing
*   Standard ansible verification
*   Custom environment variables and configuration options

Supplemental Inventory Generation[¶](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/ansible-native/#supplemental-inventory-generation "Permanent link")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

In the ansible-native approach, Molecule generates only a supplemental inventory file containing molecule-specific variables that are made available to playbooks. This includes environment variables like `MOLECULE_SCENARIO_DIRECTORY`, `MOLECULE_EPHEMERAL_DIRECTORY`, and scenario metadata. The primary inventory comes from the sources specified in `ansible.executor.args.ansible_playbook`, allowing full integration with existing inventory management systems while maintaining Molecule's testing capabilities.

Back to top

# Ansible ecosystem | Ansible documentation

Join us at [Red Hat Summit in Atlanta](https://www.redhat.com/en/summit?sc_id=RHCTE0250000464461)
 to learn about Ansible Automation Platform | May 11-14, 2026

Ansible ecosystem
=================

Expand your automation to a virtually unlimited set of use cases.

### Awesome Ansible

A collaborative curated list of awesome Ansible resources, tools, roles, tutorials and other related content.

*   [Awesome Ansible list](https://github.com/ansible-community/awesome-ansible/blob/main/README.md)
    

![Ansible community logo](https://docs.ansible.com/images/project-logos/ansible-community.svg)

### Ansible community package

The Ansible community package consists of ansible-core and a set of Ansible collections published as the Python \`ansible\` package, in tradition of the Ansible 2.9 and earlier "batteries included" releases.

*   [PyPI page for Ansible community package](https://pypi.org/project/ansible/)
    
*   [Documentation of package build process](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-build-data/)
    
*   [Source code of package build](https://github.com/ansible-community/ansible-build-data)
    
*   [Source code of the Antsibull build tool](https://github.com/ansible-community/antsibull)
    

![Ansible community logo](https://docs.ansible.com/images/project-logos/ansible-community.svg)

### Antsibull Changelog

A changelog generator used by ansible-core and Ansible collections.

*   [Documentation](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/antsibull-changelog/)
    
*   [Source code](https://github.com/ansible-community/antsibull-changelog)
    

![Ansible community logo](https://docs.ansible.com/images/project-logos/ansible-community.svg)

### Antsibull Docs

Tooling for building documentation for Ansible collections, ansible-core, and the Ansible community package.

*   [Documentation](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/antsibull-docs/)
    
*   [Source code](https://github.com/ansible-community/antsibull-docs)
    
*   [Python library for parsing Ansible markup](https://github.com/ansible-community/antsibull-docs-parser)
    
*   [TypeScript library for parsing Ansible markup](https://github.com/ansible-community/antsibull-docs-ts)
    

![Ansible community logo](https://docs.ansible.com/images/project-logos/ansible-community.svg)

### Anstibull Nox

A nox helper library that simplifies the process of testing Ansible collections through a common interface for various tools.

*   [Documentation](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/antsibull-nox/)
    
*   [Source code](https://github.com/ansible-community/antsibull-nox)
    

![Ansible community logo](https://docs.ansible.com/images/project-logos/ansible-community.svg)

### Ansible Development Environment

A pip-like install for Ansible collections that isolates environments and promotes an ephemeral approach to development.

*   [Documentation](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/dev-environment/)
    
*   [Source code](https://github.com/ansible/ansible-development-environment)
    

![Ansible Development Environment project logo](https://docs.ansible.com/images/project-logos/ansible-community.svg)

### Ansible Development Tools

Ansible Development Tools (ADT) streamlines the setup and usage of several tools for creating Ansible content.

*   [Documentation](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/dev-tools/)
    
*   [Source code](https://github.com/ansible/ansible-dev-tools)
    

![Ansible Development Tools project logo](https://docs.ansible.com/images/project-logos/ansible-community.svg)

### ansible-pylibssh

ansible-pylibssh provides Python bindings for Ansible with the libssh project.

*   [Documentation](https://ansible-pylibssh.readthedocs.io/en/latest/)
    
*   [Source code](https://github.com/ansible/pylibssh)
    

![ansible-pylibssh project logo](https://docs.ansible.com/images/project-logos/ansible-community.svg)

### Ansible AWX

AWX provides a web-based user interface, REST API, and task engine built on top of Ansible.

*   [Documentation](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/awx/)
    
*   [Source code](https://github.com/ansible/awx)
    

![AWX project logo](https://docs.ansible.com/images/project-logos/awx.svg)

### AWX Operator

Ansible AWX Operator offers built-in intelligence and operational best practices for deploying on Kubernetes environments.

*   [Documentation](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/awx-operator/)
    
*   [Source code](https://github.com/ansible/awx-operator)
    

![AWX Operator project logo](https://docs.ansible.com/images/project-logos/ansible-community.svg)

### Ansible Builder

Ansible Builder lets you create Execution Environments, which are container images that act as Ansible control nodes.

*   [Documentation](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/builder/)
    
*   [Source code](https://github.com/ansible/ansible-builder)
    

![Ansible Builder project logo](https://docs.ansible.com/images/project-logos/builder.svg)

### Ansible collections

Ansible collections offer distributions of playbooks, roles, modules, and plugins.

*   [Collection index](https://docs.ansible.com/ansible/latest/collections/index.html)
    
*   [Find out how to use collections](https://docs.ansible.com/ansible/latest/collections_guide/index.html)
    
*   [Learn how to contribute to collections](https://docs.ansible.com/ansible/devel/community/contributions_collections.html#collections-contributions)
    
*   [Index of all modules and plugins](https://docs.ansible.com/ansible/latest/collections/all_plugins.html)
    

![Ansible community logo](https://docs.ansible.com/images/project-logos/ansible-community.svg)

### Ansible Compat

Compat is a Python package that assists with compatibility between different Ansible releases, starting at version 2.9.

*   [Documentation](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/compat/)
    
*   [Source code](https://github.com/ansible/ansible-compat)
    

![Ansible Compat project logo](https://docs.ansible.com/images/project-logos/ansible-community.svg)

### Ansible Core

Ansible Core is the language and runtime that powers automation. It also provides command-line tools such as Ansible Test.

*   [Ansible Core documentation](https://docs.ansible.com/ansible-core/devel/index.html)
    
*   [Ansible Test documentation](https://docs.ansible.com/ansible-core/devel/dev_guide/testing_running_locally.html)
    
*   [Source code](https://github.com/ansible/ansible)
    

![Ansible Core project logo](https://docs.ansible.com/images/project-logos/ansible-core.svg)

### Ansible Creator

Ansible Creator is a Command-Line Interface (CLI) tool designed for effortlessly scaffolding all your Ansible content.

*   [Documentation](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/creator/)
    
*   [Source code](https://github.com/ansible/ansible-creator)
    

![Ansible Creator project logo](https://docs.ansible.com/images/project-logos/ansible-community.svg)

### Event-Driven Ansible Server

Event-Driven Ansible Server offers scalable and flexible automation that can subscribe to a wide variety of event sources.

*   [Deployment guide](https://github.com/ansible/eda-server/blob/main/docs/deployment.md)
    
*   [Development environment setup](https://github.com/ansible/eda-server/blob/main/docs/development.md)
    
*   [Source code](https://github.com/ansible/eda-server)
    

![Event-Driven Ansible Server project logo](https://docs.ansible.com/images/project-logos/eda.svg)

### Edge Automation

Edge provides tooling and collections to run automation jobs on device endpoints at the very edge of your infrastructure.

*   [Osbuild Composer Collection](https://github.com/redhat-cop/infra.osbuild)
    
*   [Common Industrial Protocol (CIP) Collection](https://github.com/ansible-collections/community.cip)
    
*   [FDO Collection](https://github.com/ansible-collections/community.fdo)
    
*   [MicroShift Collection](https://github.com/ansible-collections/edge.microshift/)
    

![Edge Automation logo](https://docs.ansible.com/images/project-logos/ansible-community.svg)

### Galaxy NG

Galaxy NG jumpstarts automation projects with Ansible community content. Galaxy lets you share Ansible automation and promotes a collaborative approach to development and maintenance.

*   [Search Galaxy](https://galaxy.ansible.com/search)
    
*   [Documentation](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/galaxy-ng/)
    
*   [Source code](https://github.com/ansible/galaxy_ng/)
    

![Galaxy NG project logo](https://docs.ansible.com/images/project-logos/galaxy.svg)

### Ansible Lint

Lint improves code quality through proven best practices, patterns, and behaviors so that your Ansible content results in reliable and consistent automation.

*   [Documentation](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/lint/)
    
*   [Source code](https://github.com/ansible/ansible-lint)
    

![Ansible Lint project logo](https://docs.ansible.com/images/project-logos/ansible-community.svg)

### Molecule

Molecule helps you develop and test Ansible roles with consistent development patterns. Using Molecule results in well-written roles that are easy to understand and maintain.

*   [Documentation](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/molecule/)
    
*   [Source code](https://github.com/ansible/molecule/)
    

![Molecule project logo](https://docs.ansible.com/images/project-logos/ansible-community.svg)

### Ansible Navigator

Ansible Navigator is a command-line tool for creating, reviewing, and troubleshooting Ansible content.

*   [Documentation](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/navigator/)
    
*   [Source code](https://github.com/ansible/ansible-navigator)
    

![Ansible Navigator project logo](https://docs.ansible.com/images/project-logos/ansible-community.svg)

### Ansible Pytest

Enables the use of Ansible in tests as well as the use of pytest as a collection unit test runner, and exposes molecule scenarios using a pytest fixture.

*   [Documentation](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/pytest-ansible/)
    
*   [Source code](https://github.com/ansible/pytest-ansible)
    

![Ansible Pytest project logo](https://docs.ansible.com/images/project-logos/ansible-community.svg)

### Ansible Rulebook

Ansible Rulebook is a command-line tool that listens to events so your automation can react when software or system states change.

*   [Documentation](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/rulebook/)
    
*   [Source code](https://github.com/ansible/ansible-rulebook)
    

![Ansible Rulebook project logo](https://docs.ansible.com/images/project-logos/ansible-community.svg)

### Ansible Runner

Ansible Runner provides a stable and consistent interface abstraction to Ansible.

*   [Documentation](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/runner/)
    
*   [Source code](https://github.com/ansible/ansible-runner)
    

![Ansible Runner project logo](https://docs.ansible.com/images/project-logos/runner.svg)

### Ansible SDK

Ansible SDK is a toolkit that lets you harness the power and simplicity of Ansible automation directly from your applications.

*   [Documentation](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/sdk/)
    
*   [Source code](https://github.com/ansible/ansible-sdk)
    

![Ansible SDK project logo](https://docs.ansible.com/images/project-logos/ansible-community.svg)

### Ansible Sign

Ansible Sign is a utility for signing and verifying Ansible automation. Use Sign to secure workflows and pipelines for trusted automation content.

*   [Documentation](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/sign/)
    
*   [Source code](https://github.com/ansible/ansible-sign)
    

![Ansible Sign project logo](https://docs.ansible.com/images/project-logos/ansible-community.svg)

### Tox Ansible

Tox Ansible is a utility designed to simplify the testing of Ansible content collections.

*   [Documentation](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/tox-ansible/)
    
*   [Source code](https://github.com/ansible/tox-ansible)
    

![Tox Ansible project logo](https://docs.ansible.com/images/project-logos/ansible-community.svg)

### Ansible VS Code Extension

The VS Code extension adds Ansible language support to Visual Studio Code and OpenVSX compatible editors.

*   [Documentation](https://marketplace.visualstudio.com/items?itemName=redhat.ansible)
    
*   [Source code](https://github.com/ansible/vscode-ansible)
    

![Ansible VS Code Extension project logo](https://docs.ansible.com/images/project-logos/ansible-community.svg)

*   [CC BY-SA 4.0](https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-sa/4.0/)

# Interpreter Discovery — Ansible Core Documentation

*   [Blog](https://www.ansible.com/blog)
    
*   [Ansible community forum](https://forum.ansible.com/)
    
*   [Documentation](https://docs.ansible.com/)
    

[![Ansible Logo](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/_static/images/Ansible-Mark-RGB_White.png)\
\
Ansible Core Documentation](https://docs.ansible.com/)

*   [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/index.html)
    
*   Interpreter Discovery
*   [Edit on GitHub](https://github.com/ansible/ansible-documentation/edit/devel/docs/docsite/rst/reference_appendices/interpreter_discovery.rst?description=%23%23%23%23%23%20SUMMARY%0A%3C!---%20Your%20description%20here%20--%3E%0A%0A%0A%23%23%23%23%23%20ISSUE%20TYPE%0A-%20Docs%20Pull%20Request%0A%0A%2Blabel:%20docsite_pr)
    

* * *

Interpreter Discovery[](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/reference_appendices/interpreter_discovery.html#interpreter-discovery "Link to this heading")

============================================================================================================================================================================

Most Ansible modules that execute under a POSIX environment require a Python interpreter on the target host. Unless configured otherwise, Ansible will attempt to discover a suitable Python interpreter on each target host the first time a Python module is executed for that host.

To control the discovery behavior:

*   for individual hosts and groups, use the `ansible_python_interpreter` inventory variable
    
*   globally, use the `interpreter_python` key in the `[defaults]` section of `ansible.cfg`
    

Configure a path to a specific Python interpreter, or one of the following values:

auto (default) :

Searches the configurable list of common Python interpreter paths (see [INTERPRETER\_PYTHON\_FALLBACK](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/reference_appendices/config.html#interpreter-python-fallback)
) and issues a warning that future installation of another Python interpreter could alter the one chosen.

auto\_legacy :

Deprecated alias for `auto`.

auto\_silent :

Same as `auto`, but does not issue warnings.

auto\_legacy\_silent :

Deprecated alias for `auto_silent`.

---

\
Ansible Core Documentation](https://docs.ansible.com/)

*   [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/index.html)
    
*   Red Hat Ansible Automation Platform
*   [Edit on GitHub](https://github.com/ansible/ansible-documentation/edit/devel/docs/docsite/rst/reference_appendices/tower.rst?description=%23%23%23%23%23%20SUMMARY%0A%3C!---%20Your%20description%20here%20--%3E%0A%0A%0A%23%23%23%23%23%20ISSUE%20TYPE%0A-%20Docs%20Pull%20Request%0A%0A%2Blabel:%20docsite_pr)
    

* * *

Red Hat Ansible Automation Platform[](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/reference_appendices/tower.html#red-hat-ansible-automation-platform "Link to this heading")

========================================================================================================================================================================================

Important

Red Hat Ansible Automation Platform is available on multiple cloud platforms. See [Ansible on Clouds](https://access.redhat.com/documentation/en-us/ansible_on_clouds/2.x)
 for details.

[Red Hat Ansible Automation Platform](https://www.ansible.com/products/automation-platform)
 (RHAAP) is an integrated solution for operationalizing Ansible across your team, organization, and enterprise. The platform includes a controller with a web console and REST API, analytics, Execution Environments, and much more.

RHAAP gives you role-based access control, including control over the use of securely stored credentials for SSH and other services. You can sync your inventory with a wide variety of cloud sources, and powerful multi-playbook workflows allow you to model complex processes.

RHAAP logs all of your jobs, integrates well with LDAP, SAML, and other authentication sources, and has an amazing browsable REST API. Command line tools are available for easy integration with Jenkins as well.

RHAAP incorporates the downstream Red Hat supported product version of Ansible AWX, the downstream Red Hat supported product version of Ansible Galaxy, and multiple SaaS offerings. Find out more about RHAAP features and on the [Red Hat Ansible Automation Platform webpage](https://www.ansible.com/products/automation-platform)
. A Red Hat Ansible Automation Platform subscription includes support from Red Hat, Inc.

---

Ansible Core Documentation](https://docs.ansible.com/)

*   [Edit on GitHub](https://github.com/ansible/ansible-documentation/edit/devel/docs/docsite/rst/reference_appendices/automationhub.rst?description=%23%23%23%23%23%20SUMMARY%0A%3C!---%20Your%20description%20here%20--%3E%0A%0A%0A%23%23%23%23%23%20ISSUE%20TYPE%0A-%20Docs%20Pull%20Request%0A%0A%2Blabel:%20docsite_pr)
    

* * *

Ansible Automation Hub[](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/reference_appendices/automationhub.html#ansible-automation-hub "Link to this heading")

======================================================================================================================================================================

[Ansible Automation Hub](https://www.ansible.com/products/automation-hub)
 is the official location to discover and download certified [collections](https://catalog.redhat.com/software/search?type=Ansible%20Collection&p=1)
, included as part of an Ansible Automation Platform subscription. These content collections contain modules, plugins, roles, and playbooks in a downloadable package.

Ansible Automation Hub gives you direct access to trusted content collections from Red Hat and Certified Partners. You can find content by topic or Ansible Partner organizations.

Ansible Automation Hub is the downstream Red Hat supported product version of Ansible Galaxy. Find out more about Ansible Automation Hub features and how to access it at [Ansible Automation Hub](https://www.ansible.com/products/automation-hub)
. Ansible Automation Hub is part of the Red Hat Ansible Automation Platform subscription, and comes bundled with support from Red Hat, Inc.

# Logging Ansible output — Ansible Core Documentation

*   [Blog](https://www.ansible.com/blog)
    
*   [Ansible community forum](https://forum.ansible.com/)
    
*   [Documentation](https://docs.ansible.com/)
    

[![Ansible Logo](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/_static/images/Ansible-Mark-RGB_White.png)\
\
Ansible Core Documentation](https://docs.ansible.com/)

*   [](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/index.html)
    
*   Logging Ansible output
*   [Edit on GitHub](https://github.com/ansible/ansible-documentation/edit/devel/docs/docsite/rst/reference_appendices/logging.rst?description=%23%23%23%23%23%20SUMMARY%0A%3C!---%20Your%20description%20here%20--%3E%0A%0A%0A%23%23%23%23%23%20ISSUE%20TYPE%0A-%20Docs%20Pull%20Request%0A%0A%2Blabel:%20docsite_pr)
    

* * *

Logging Ansible output[](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/reference_appendices/logging.html#logging-ansible-output "Link to this heading")

================================================================================================================================================================

By default, Ansible sends output about plays, tasks, and module arguments to your screen (STDOUT) on the control node. If you want to capture Ansible output in a log, you have three options:

*   To save Ansible output in a single log on the control node, set the `log_path` [configuration file setting](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/installation_guide/intro_configuration.html#intro-configuration)
    . You may also want to set `display_args_to_stdout`, which helps to differentiate similar tasks by including variable values in the Ansible output.
    
*   To save Ansible output in separate logs, one on each managed node, set the `no_target_syslog` and `syslog_facility` [configuration file settings](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/installation_guide/intro_configuration.html#intro-configuration)
    .
    
*   To save Ansible output to a secure database, use AWX or [Red Hat Ansible Automation Platform](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/reference_appendices/tower.html#ansible-platform)
    . You can then review history based on hosts, projects, and particular inventories over time, using graphs and/or a REST API.
    

Protecting sensitive data with `no_log`[](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/reference_appendices/logging.html#protecting-sensitive-data-with-no-log "Link to this heading")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

If you save Ansible output to a log, you expose any secret data in your Ansible output, such as passwords and usernames. To keep sensitive values out of your logs, mark tasks that expose them with the `no_log: True` attribute. However, the `no_log` attribute does not affect debugging output, so be careful not to debug playbooks in a production environment. See [How do I keep secret data in my playbook?](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/reference_appendices/faq.html#keep-secret-data)
 for an example.

---

Note

**Making Open Source More Inclusive**

Red Hat is committed to replacing problematic language in our code, documentation, and web properties. We are beginning with these four terms: master, slave, blacklist, and whitelist. We ask that you open an issue or pull request if you come upon a term that we have missed. For more details, see [our CTO Chris Wright’s message](https://www.redhat.com/en/blog/making-open-source-more-inclusive-eradicating-problematic-language)
.

* * *

Sanity Tests[](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/dev_guide/testing/sanity/index.html#sanity-tests "Link to this heading")

==============================================================================================================================================

The following sanity tests are available as `--test` options for `ansible-test sanity` when testing Ansible Collections. This list is also available using `ansible-test sanity --list-tests --allow-disabled`.

For information on how to run these tests, see [sanity testing guide](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/dev_guide/testing_sanity.html#testing-sanity)
.

*   [action-plugin-docs](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/dev_guide/testing/sanity/action-plugin-docs.html)
    
*   [ansible-doc](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/dev_guide/testing/sanity/ansible-doc.html)
    
*   [changelog](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/dev_guide/testing/sanity/changelog.html)
    
*   [compile](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/dev_guide/testing/sanity/compile.html)
    
*   [empty-init](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/dev_guide/testing/sanity/empty-init.html)
    
*   [ignores](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/dev_guide/testing/sanity/ignores.html)
    
*   [import](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/dev_guide/testing/sanity/import.html)
    
*   [line-endings](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/dev_guide/testing/sanity/line-endings.html)
    
*   [no-assert](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/dev_guide/testing/sanity/no-assert.html)
    
*   [no-basestring](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/dev_guide/testing/sanity/no-basestring.html)
    
*   [no-dict-iteritems](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/dev_guide/testing/sanity/no-dict-iteritems.html)
    
*   [no-dict-iterkeys](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/dev_guide/testing/sanity/no-dict-iterkeys.html)
    
*   [no-dict-itervalues](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/dev_guide/testing/sanity/no-dict-itervalues.html)
    
*   [no-get-exception](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/dev_guide/testing/sanity/no-get-exception.html)
    
*   [no-illegal-filenames](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/dev_guide/testing/sanity/no-illegal-filenames.html)
    
*   [no-main-display](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/dev_guide/testing/sanity/no-main-display.html)
    
*   [no-smart-quotes](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/dev_guide/testing/sanity/no-smart-quotes.html)
    
*   [no-unicode-literals](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/dev_guide/testing/sanity/no-unicode-literals.html)
    
*   [pep8](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/dev_guide/testing/sanity/pep8.html)
    
*   [pslint](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/dev_guide/testing/sanity/pslint.html)
    
*   [pylint](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/dev_guide/testing/sanity/pylint.html)
    
*   [replace-urlopen](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/dev_guide/testing/sanity/replace-urlopen.html)
    
*   [runtime-metadata](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/dev_guide/testing/sanity/runtime-metadata.html)
    
*   [shebang](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/dev_guide/testing/sanity/shebang.html)

*   [symlinks](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/dev_guide/testing/sanity/symlinks.html)

Additional tests are available when testing Ansible Core:

*   [ansible-requirements](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/dev_guide/testing/sanity/ansible-requirements.html)
