ANSIBLE\_CALLBACK\_RESULT\_FORMAT[](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/environment_variables.html#envvar-ANSIBLE_CALLBACK_RESULT_FORMAT "Link to this definition")

Define the task result format used in the callback output.

These formats do not cause the callback to emit valid JSON or YAML formats.

The output contains these formats interspersed with other non-machine parsable data.

_Used by:_ [ansible.builtin.default callback plugin](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/default_callback.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-default-callback)
, [ansible.builtin.minimal callback plugin](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/minimal_callback.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-minimal-callback)

ANSIBLE\_CALLBACK\_RESULT\_INDENTATION[](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/environment_variables.html#envvar-ANSIBLE_CALLBACK_RESULT_INDENTATION "Link to this definition")

Allows to configure indentation for YAML and verbose/pretty JSON.

Please note that for `result_format=yaml`, only values between 2 and 9 will be handled as expected by PyYAML. If indentation is set to 1, or to 10 or larger, the first level of indentation will be used, but all further indentations will be by 2 spaces.

_Used by:_ [ansible.builtin.default callback plugin](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/default_callback.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-default-callback)
, [ansible.builtin.minimal callback plugin](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/minimal_callback.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-minimal-callback)

ANSIBLE\_CALLBACK\_TREE\_DIR[](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/environment_variables.html#envvar-ANSIBLE_CALLBACK_TREE_DIR "Link to this definition")

directory that will contain the per host JSON files. Also set by the `--tree` option when using adhoc.

_Used by:_ [ansible.builtin.tree callback plugin](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/tree_callback.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-tree-callback)

ANSIBLE\_CALLBACK\_YAML\_LINE\_WIDTH[](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/environment_variables.html#envvar-ANSIBLE_CALLBACK_YAML_LINE_WIDTH "Link to this definition")

Configure the line width used for YAML. The YAML serializer will try to break longer lines.

_Used by:_ [ansible.builtin.default callback plugin](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/default_callback.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-default-callback)
, [ansible.builtin.minimal callback plugin](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/minimal_callback.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-minimal-callback)

ANSIBLE\_CHECK\_MODE\_MARKERS[](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/environment_variables.html#envvar-ANSIBLE_CHECK_MODE_MARKERS "Link to this definition")

Toggle to control displaying markers when running in check mode.

The markers are `DRY RUN` at the beginning and ending of playbook execution (when calling `ansible-playbook --check`) and `CHECK MODE` as a suffix at every play and task that is run in check mode.

_Used by:_ [ansible.builtin.default callback plugin](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/default_callback.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-default-callback)

ANSIBLE\_COMMON\_REMOTE\_GROUP[](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/environment_variables.html#envvar-ANSIBLE_COMMON_REMOTE_GROUP "Link to this definition")

Checked when Ansible needs to execute a module as a different user.

If setfacl and chown both fail and do not let the different user access the module’s files, they will be chgrp’d to this group.

In order for this to work, the remote\_user and become\_user must share a common group and this setting must be set to that group.

_Used by:_ [ansible.builtin.sh shell plugin](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/sh_shell.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-sh-shell)

ANSIBLE\_DISPLAY\_FAILED\_STDERR[](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/environment_variables.html#envvar-ANSIBLE_DISPLAY_FAILED_STDERR "Link to this definition")

Toggle to control whether failed and unreachable tasks are displayed to STDERR rather than STDOUT.

_Used by:_ [ansible.builtin.default callback plugin](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/default_callback.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-default-callback)

ANSIBLE\_DISPLAY\_INCLUDED\_HOSTS[](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/environment_variables.html#envvar-ANSIBLE_DISPLAY_INCLUDED_HOSTS "Link to this definition")

Toggle to control displaying included task/host results in a task.

_Used by:_ [ansible.builtin.default callback plugin](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/default_callback.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-default-callback)

ANSIBLE\_DISPLAY\_OK\_HOSTS[](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/environment_variables.html#envvar-ANSIBLE_DISPLAY_OK_HOSTS "Link to this definition")

Toggle to control displaying ‘ok’ task/host results in a task.

_Used by:_ [ansible.builtin.default callback plugin](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/default_callback.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-default-callback)

ANSIBLE\_GENERATOR\_USE\_EXTRA\_VARS[](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/environment_variables.html#envvar-ANSIBLE_GENERATOR_USE_EXTRA_VARS "Link to this definition")

Merge extra vars into the available variables for composition (highest precedence).

_Used by:_ [ansible.builtin.generator inventory plugin](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/generator_inventory.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-generator-inventory)

ANSIBLE\_INVENTORY\_PLUGIN\_EXTS[](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/environment_variables.html#envvar-ANSIBLE_INVENTORY_PLUGIN_EXTS "Link to this definition")

list of ‘valid’ extensions for files containing YAML

_Used by:_ [ansible.builtin.yaml inventory plugin](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/yaml_inventory.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-yaml-inventory)

ANSIBLE\_INVENTORY\_PLUGIN\_SCRIPT\_STDERR[](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/environment_variables.html#envvar-ANSIBLE_INVENTORY_PLUGIN_SCRIPT_STDERR "Link to this definition")

Toggle display of stderr even when script was successful

_Used by:_ [ansible.builtin.script inventory plugin](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/script_inventory.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-script-inventory)

ANSIBLE\_INVENTORY\_USE\_EXTRA\_VARS[](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/environment_variables.html#envvar-ANSIBLE_INVENTORY_USE_EXTRA_VARS "Link to this definition")

Merge extra vars into the available variables for composition (highest precedence).

_Used by:_ [ansible.builtin.constructed inventory plugin](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/constructed_inventory.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-constructed-inventory)
, [ansible.builtin.generator inventory plugin](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/generator_inventory.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-generator-inventory)

ANSIBLE\_LOOKUP\_URL\_AGENT[](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/environment_variables.html#envvar-ANSIBLE_LOOKUP_URL_AGENT "Link to this definition")

User-Agent to use in the request. The default was changed in 2.11 to `ansible-httpget`.

_Used by:_ [ansible.builtin.url lookup plugin](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/url_lookup.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-url-lookup)

ANSIBLE\_LOOKUP\_URL\_CA\_PATH[](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/environment_variables.html#envvar-ANSIBLE_LOOKUP_URL_CA_PATH "Link to this definition")

String of file system path to CA cert bundle to use

_Used by:_ [ansible.builtin.url lookup plugin](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/url_lookup.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-url-lookup)

ANSIBLE\_LOOKUP\_URL\_CIPHERS[](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/environment_variables.html#envvar-ANSIBLE_LOOKUP_URL_CIPHERS "Link to this definition")

SSL/TLS Ciphers to use for the request

When a list is provided, all ciphers are joined in order with `:`

See the [OpenSSL Cipher List Format](https://docs.openssl.org/master/man1/openssl-ciphers/#cipher-list-format)
 for more details.

The available ciphers is dependent on the Python and OpenSSL/LibreSSL versions

_Used by:_ [ansible.builtin.url lookup plugin](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/url_lookup.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-url-lookup)

ANSIBLE\_LOOKUP\_URL\_FOLLOW\_REDIRECTS[](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/environment_variables.html#envvar-ANSIBLE_LOOKUP_URL_FOLLOW_REDIRECTS "Link to this definition")

Whether or not the URI module should follow redirects.

_Used by:_ [ansible.builtin.url lookup plugin](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/url_lookup.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-url-lookup)

ANSIBLE\_LOOKUP\_URL\_FORCE[](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/environment_variables.html#envvar-ANSIBLE_LOOKUP_URL_FORCE "Link to this definition")

Whether or not to set “cache-control” header with value “no-cache”

_Used by:_ [ansible.builtin.url lookup plugin](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/url_lookup.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-url-lookup)

ANSIBLE\_LOOKUP\_URL\_FORCE\_BASIC\_AUTH[](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/environment_variables.html#envvar-ANSIBLE_LOOKUP_URL_FORCE_BASIC_AUTH "Link to this definition")

Force basic authentication

_Used by:_ [ansible.builtin.url lookup plugin](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/url_lookup.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-url-lookup)

ANSIBLE\_LOOKUP\_URL\_TIMEOUT[](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/environment_variables.html#envvar-ANSIBLE_LOOKUP_URL_TIMEOUT "Link to this definition")

How long to wait for the server to send data before giving up

_Used by:_ [ansible.builtin.url lookup plugin](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/url_lookup.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-url-lookup)

ANSIBLE\_LOOKUP\_URL\_UNIX\_SOCKET[](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/environment_variables.html#envvar-ANSIBLE_LOOKUP_URL_UNIX_SOCKET "Link to this definition")

String of file system path to unix socket file to use when establishing connection to the provided url

_Used by:_ [ansible.builtin.url lookup plugin](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/url_lookup.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-url-lookup)

ANSIBLE\_LOOKUP\_URL\_UNREDIR\_HEADERS[](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/environment_variables.html#envvar-ANSIBLE_LOOKUP_URL_UNREDIR_HEADERS "Link to this definition")

A list of headers to not attach on a redirected request

_Used by:_ [ansible.builtin.url lookup plugin](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/url_lookup.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-url-lookup)

ANSIBLE\_LOOKUP\_URL\_USE\_GSSAPI[](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/environment_variables.html#envvar-ANSIBLE_LOOKUP_URL_USE_GSSAPI "Link to this definition")

Use GSSAPI handler of requests

As of Ansible 2.11, GSSAPI credentials can be specified with `**username**` and `**password**`.

_Used by:_ [ansible.builtin.url lookup plugin](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/url_lookup.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-url-lookup)

ANSIBLE\_LOOKUP\_URL\_USE\_NETRC[](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/environment_variables.html#envvar-ANSIBLE_LOOKUP_URL_USE_NETRC "Link to this definition")

Determining whether to use credentials from \`\`~/.netrc\`\` file

By default .netrc is used with Basic authentication headers

When set to False, .netrc credentials are ignored

_Used by:_ [ansible.builtin.url lookup plugin](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/url_lookup.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-url-lookup)

ANSIBLE\_PARAMIKO\_BANNER\_TIMEOUT[](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/environment_variables.html#envvar-ANSIBLE_PARAMIKO_BANNER_TIMEOUT "Link to this definition")

Configures, in seconds, the amount of time to wait for the SSH banner to be presented. This option is supported by paramiko version 1.15.0 or newer.

_Used by:_ [ansible.builtin.paramiko\_ssh connection plugin](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/paramiko_ssh_connection.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-paramiko-ssh-connection)

ANSIBLE\_PARAMIKO\_HOST\_KEY\_AUTO\_ADD[](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/environment_variables.html#envvar-ANSIBLE_PARAMIKO_HOST_KEY_AUTO_ADD "Link to this definition")

Automatically add host keys

_Used by:_ [ansible.builtin.paramiko\_ssh connection plugin](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/paramiko_ssh_connection.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-paramiko-ssh-connection)

ANSIBLE\_PARAMIKO\_HOST\_KEY\_CHECKING[](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/environment_variables.html#envvar-ANSIBLE_PARAMIKO_HOST_KEY_CHECKING "Link to this definition")

Set this to “False” if you want to avoid host key checking by the underlying tools Ansible uses to connect to the host

_Used by:_ [ansible.builtin.paramiko\_ssh connection plugin](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/paramiko_ssh_connection.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-paramiko-ssh-connection)

ANSIBLE\_PARAMIKO\_LOOK\_FOR\_KEYS[](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/environment_variables.html#envvar-ANSIBLE_PARAMIKO_LOOK_FOR_KEYS "Link to this definition")

False to disable searching for private key files in ~/.ssh/

_Used by:_ [ansible.builtin.paramiko\_ssh connection plugin](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/paramiko_ssh_connection.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-paramiko-ssh-connection)

ANSIBLE\_PARAMIKO\_PRIVATE\_KEY\_FILE[](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/environment_variables.html#envvar-ANSIBLE_PARAMIKO_PRIVATE_KEY_FILE "Link to this definition")

Path to private key file to use for authentication.

_Used by:_ [ansible.builtin.paramiko\_ssh connection plugin](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/paramiko_ssh_connection.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-paramiko-ssh-connection)

ANSIBLE\_PARAMIKO\_PROXY\_COMMAND[](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/environment_variables.html#envvar-ANSIBLE_PARAMIKO_PROXY_COMMAND "Link to this definition")

Proxy information for running the connection via a jumphost.

_Used by:_ [ansible.builtin.paramiko\_ssh connection plugin](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/paramiko_ssh_connection.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-paramiko-ssh-connection)

ANSIBLE\_PARAMIKO\_PTY[](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/environment_variables.html#envvar-ANSIBLE_PARAMIKO_PTY "Link to this definition")

SUDO usually requires a PTY, True to give a PTY and False to not give a PTY.

_Used by:_ [ansible.builtin.paramiko\_ssh connection plugin](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/paramiko_ssh_connection.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-paramiko-ssh-connection)

ANSIBLE\_PARAMIKO\_RECORD\_HOST\_KEYS[](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/environment_variables.html#envvar-ANSIBLE_PARAMIKO_RECORD_HOST_KEYS "Link to this definition")

Save the host keys to a file

_Used by:_ [ansible.builtin.paramiko\_ssh connection plugin](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/paramiko_ssh_connection.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-paramiko-ssh-connection)

ANSIBLE\_PARAMIKO\_REMOTE\_USER[](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/environment_variables.html#envvar-ANSIBLE_PARAMIKO_REMOTE_USER "Link to this definition")

User to login/authenticate as

Can be set from the CLI via the `--user` or `-u` options.

_Used by:_ [ansible.builtin.paramiko\_ssh connection plugin](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/paramiko_ssh_connection.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-paramiko-ssh-connection)

ANSIBLE\_PARAMIKO\_TIMEOUT[](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/environment_variables.html#envvar-ANSIBLE_PARAMIKO_TIMEOUT "Link to this definition")

Number of seconds until the plugin gives up on failing to establish a TCP connection.

_Used by:_ [ansible.builtin.paramiko\_ssh connection plugin](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/paramiko_ssh_connection.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-paramiko-ssh-connection)

ANSIBLE\_PARAMIKO\_USE\_RSA\_SHA2\_ALGORITHMS[](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/environment_variables.html#envvar-ANSIBLE_PARAMIKO_USE_RSA_SHA2_ALGORITHMS "Link to this definition")

Whether or not to enable RSA SHA2 algorithms for pubkeys and hostkeys

On paramiko versions older than 2.9, this only affects hostkeys

For behavior matching paramiko<2.9 set this to `False`

_Used by:_ [ansible.builtin.paramiko\_ssh connection plugin](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/paramiko_ssh_connection.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-paramiko-ssh-connection)

ANSIBLE\_PKCS11\_PROVIDER[](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/environment_variables.html#envvar-ANSIBLE_PKCS11_PROVIDER "Link to this definition")

PKCS11 SmartCard provider such as opensc, example: /usr/local/lib/opensc-pkcs11.so

_Used by:_ [ansible.builtin.ssh connection plugin](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/ssh_connection.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-ssh-connection)

ANSIBLE\_PRIVATE\_KEY[](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/environment_variables.html#envvar-ANSIBLE_PRIVATE_KEY "Link to this definition")

Private key contents in PEM format. Requires the `SSH_AGENT` configuration to be enabled.

_Used by:_ [ansible.builtin.ssh connection plugin](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/ssh_connection.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-ssh-connection)

ANSIBLE\_PRIVATE\_KEY\_PASSPHRASE[](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/environment_variables.html#envvar-ANSIBLE_PRIVATE_KEY_PASSPHRASE "Link to this definition")

Private key passphrase, dependent on `**private_key**`.

This does NOT have any effect when used with `**private_key_file**`.

_Used by:_ [ansible.builtin.ssh connection plugin](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/ssh_connection.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-ssh-connection)

ANSIBLE\_REMOTE\_PARAMIKO\_PORT[](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/environment_variables.html#envvar-ANSIBLE_REMOTE_PARAMIKO_PORT "Link to this definition")

Remote port to connect to.

_Used by:_ [ansible.builtin.paramiko\_ssh connection plugin](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/paramiko_ssh_connection.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-paramiko-ssh-connection)

ANSIBLE\_REMOTE\_TEMP[](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/environment_variables.html#envvar-ANSIBLE_REMOTE_TEMP "Link to this definition")

Temporary directory to use on targets when executing tasks.

_Used by:_ [ansible.builtin.sh shell plugin](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/sh_shell.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-sh-shell)

ANSIBLE\_REMOTE\_TMP[](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/environment_variables.html#envvar-ANSIBLE_REMOTE_TMP "Link to this definition")

Temporary directory to use on targets when executing tasks.

_Used by:_ [ansible.builtin.sh shell plugin](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/sh_shell.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-sh-shell)

ANSIBLE\_RUNAS\_FLAGS[](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/environment_variables.html#envvar-ANSIBLE_RUNAS_FLAGS "Link to this definition")

Options to pass to runas, a space delimited list of k=v pairs

_Used by:_ [ansible.builtin.runas become plugin](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/runas_become.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-runas-become)

ANSIBLE\_RUNAS\_PASS[](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/environment_variables.html#envvar-ANSIBLE_RUNAS_PASS "Link to this definition")

password

_Used by:_ [ansible.builtin.runas become plugin](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/runas_become.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-runas-become)

ANSIBLE\_RUNAS\_USER[](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/environment_variables.html#envvar-ANSIBLE_RUNAS_USER "Link to this definition")

User you ‘become’ to execute the task

_Used by:_ [ansible.builtin.runas become plugin](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/runas_become.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-runas-become)

ANSIBLE\_SCP\_EXECUTABLE[](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/environment_variables.html#envvar-ANSIBLE_SCP_EXECUTABLE "Link to this definition")

This defines the location of the scp binary. It defaults to `scp` which will use the first binary available in $PATH.

_Used by:_ [ansible.builtin.ssh connection plugin](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/ssh_connection.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-ssh-connection)

ANSIBLE\_SCP\_EXTRA\_ARGS[](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/environment_variables.html#envvar-ANSIBLE_SCP_EXTRA_ARGS "Link to this definition")

Extra exclusive to the `scp` CLI

_Used by:_ [ansible.builtin.ssh connection plugin](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/ssh_connection.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-ssh-connection)

ANSIBLE\_SFTP\_BATCH\_MODE[](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/environment_variables.html#envvar-ANSIBLE_SFTP_BATCH_MODE "Link to this definition")

When set to `True`, sftp will be run in batch mode, allowing detection of transfer errors.

When set to `False`, sftp will not be run in batch mode, preventing detection of transfer errors.

_Used by:_ [ansible.builtin.ssh connection plugin](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/ssh_connection.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-ssh-connection)

ANSIBLE\_SFTP\_EXECUTABLE[](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/environment_variables.html#envvar-ANSIBLE_SFTP_EXECUTABLE "Link to this definition")

This defines the location of the sftp binary. It defaults to `sftp` which will use the first binary available in $PATH.

_Used by:_ [ansible.builtin.ssh connection plugin](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/ssh_connection.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-ssh-connection)

ANSIBLE\_SFTP\_EXTRA\_ARGS[](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/environment_variables.html#envvar-ANSIBLE_SFTP_EXTRA_ARGS "Link to this definition")

Extra exclusive to the `sftp` CLI

_Used by:_ [ansible.builtin.ssh connection plugin](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/ssh_connection.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-ssh-connection)

ANSIBLE\_SHELL\_ALLOW\_WORLD\_READABLE\_TEMP[](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/environment_variables.html#envvar-ANSIBLE_SHELL_ALLOW_WORLD_READABLE_TEMP "Link to this definition")

This makes the temporary files created on the machine world-readable and will issue a warning instead of failing the task.

It is useful when becoming an unprivileged user.

_Used by:_ [ansible.builtin.sh shell plugin](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/sh_shell.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-sh-shell)

ANSIBLE\_SHOW\_PER\_HOST\_START[](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/environment_variables.html#envvar-ANSIBLE_SHOW_PER_HOST_START "Link to this definition")

This adds output that shows when a task starts to execute for each host.

_Used by:_ [ansible.builtin.default callback plugin](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/default_callback.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-default-callback)

ANSIBLE\_SHOW\_TASK\_PATH\_ON\_FAILURE[](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/environment_variables.html#envvar-ANSIBLE_SHOW_TASK_PATH_ON_FAILURE "Link to this definition")

When a task fails, display the path to the file containing the failed task and the line number. This information is displayed automatically for every task when running with `-vv` or greater verbosity.

_Used by:_ [ansible.builtin.default callback plugin](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/default_callback.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-default-callback)

ANSIBLE\_SSH\_ARGS[](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/environment_variables.html#envvar-ANSIBLE_SSH_ARGS "Link to this definition")

Arguments to pass to all SSH CLI tools.

_Used by:_ [ansible.builtin.ssh connection plugin](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/ssh_connection.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-ssh-connection)

ANSIBLE\_SSH\_COMMON\_ARGS[](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/environment_variables.html#envvar-ANSIBLE_SSH_COMMON_ARGS "Link to this definition")

Common extra args for all SSH CLI tools.

_Used by:_ [ansible.builtin.ssh connection plugin](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/ssh_connection.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-ssh-connection)

ANSIBLE\_SSH\_CONTROL\_PATH[](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/environment_variables.html#envvar-ANSIBLE_SSH_CONTROL_PATH "Link to this definition")

This is the location to save SSH’s ControlPath sockets, it uses SSH’s variable substitution.

Since 2.3, if null (default), ansible will generate a unique hash. Use \`\`%(directory)s\`\` to indicate where to use the control dir path setting.

Before 2.3 it defaulted to \`\`control\_path=%(directory)s/ansible-ssh-%%h-%%p-%%r\`\`.

Be aware that this setting is ignored if `-o ControlPath` is set in ssh args.

_Used by:_ [ansible.builtin.ssh connection plugin](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/ssh_connection.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-ssh-connection)

ANSIBLE\_SSH\_CONTROL\_PATH\_DIR[](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/environment_variables.html#envvar-ANSIBLE_SSH_CONTROL_PATH_DIR "Link to this definition")

This sets the directory to use for ssh control path if the control path setting is null.

Also, provides the \`\`%(directory)s\`\` variable for the control path setting.

_Used by:_ [ansible.builtin.ssh connection plugin](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/ssh_connection.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-ssh-connection)

ANSIBLE\_SSH\_EXECUTABLE[](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/environment_variables.html#envvar-ANSIBLE_SSH_EXECUTABLE "Link to this definition")

This defines the location of the SSH binary. It defaults to `ssh` which will use the first SSH binary available in $PATH.

This option is usually not required, it might be useful when access to system SSH is restricted, or when using SSH wrappers to connect to remote hosts.

_Used by:_ [ansible.builtin.ssh connection plugin](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/ssh_connection.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-ssh-connection)

ANSIBLE\_SSH\_EXTRA\_ARGS[](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/environment_variables.html#envvar-ANSIBLE_SSH_EXTRA_ARGS "Link to this definition")

Extra exclusive to the SSH CLI.

_Used by:_ [ansible.builtin.ssh connection plugin](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/ssh_connection.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-ssh-connection)

ANSIBLE\_SSH\_HOST\_KEY\_CHECKING[](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/environment_variables.html#envvar-ANSIBLE_SSH_HOST_KEY_CHECKING "Link to this definition")

See the documentations for the options where this environment variable is used.

_Used by:_ [ansible.builtin.paramiko\_ssh connection plugin](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/paramiko_ssh_connection.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-paramiko-ssh-connection)
, [ansible.builtin.ssh connection plugin](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/ssh_connection.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-ssh-connection)

ANSIBLE\_SSH\_PASSWORD\_MECHANISM[](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/environment_variables.html#envvar-ANSIBLE_SSH_PASSWORD_MECHANISM "Link to this definition")

Mechanism to use for handling ssh password prompt

_Used by:_ [ansible.builtin.ssh connection plugin](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/ssh_connection.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-ssh-connection)

ANSIBLE\_SSH\_PIPELINING[](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/environment_variables.html#envvar-ANSIBLE_SSH_PIPELINING "Link to this definition")

Pipelining reduces the number of connection operations required to execute a module on the remote server, by executing many Ansible modules without actual file transfers.

This can result in a very significant performance improvement when enabled.

However this can conflict with privilege escalation (`become`). For example, when using sudo operations you must first disable `requiretty` in the sudoers file for the target hosts, which is why this feature is disabled by default.

_Used by:_ [ansible.builtin.ssh connection plugin](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/ssh_connection.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-ssh-connection)

ANSIBLE\_SSH\_RETRIES[](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/environment_variables.html#envvar-ANSIBLE_SSH_RETRIES "Link to this definition")

Number of attempts to connect.

Ansible retries connections only if it gets an SSH error with a return code of 255.

Any errors with return codes other than 255 indicate an issue with program execution.

_Used by:_ [ansible.builtin.ssh connection plugin](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/ssh_connection.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-ssh-connection)

ANSIBLE\_SSH\_TIMEOUT[](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/environment_variables.html#envvar-ANSIBLE_SSH_TIMEOUT "Link to this definition")

See the documentations for the options where this environment variable is used.

_Used by:_ [ansible.builtin.paramiko\_ssh connection plugin](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/paramiko_ssh_connection.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-paramiko-ssh-connection)
, [ansible.builtin.ssh connection plugin](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/ssh_connection.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-ssh-connection)

ANSIBLE\_SSH\_TRANSFER\_METHOD[](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/environment_variables.html#envvar-ANSIBLE_SSH_TRANSFER_METHOD "Link to this definition")

Preferred method to use when transferring files over ssh

_Used by:_ [ansible.builtin.ssh connection plugin](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/ssh_connection.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-ssh-connection)

ANSIBLE\_SSH\_USETTY[](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/environment_variables.html#envvar-ANSIBLE_SSH_USETTY "Link to this definition")

add -tt to ssh commands to force tty allocation.

_Used by:_ [ansible.builtin.ssh connection plugin](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/ssh_connection.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-ssh-connection)

ANSIBLE\_SSH\_VERBOSITY[](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/environment_variables.html#envvar-ANSIBLE_SSH_VERBOSITY "Link to this definition")

Requested verbosity level for the SSH CLI.

_Used by:_ [ansible.builtin.ssh connection plugin](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/ssh_connection.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-ssh-connection)

ANSIBLE\_SSHPASS\_PROMPT[](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/environment_variables.html#envvar-ANSIBLE_SSHPASS_PROMPT "Link to this definition")

Password prompt that `sshpass`/`SSH_ASKPASS` should search for.

Supported by sshpass 1.06 and up when `**password_mechanism**` set to `sshpass`.

Defaults to `Enter PIN for` when pkcs11\_provider is set.

Defaults to `assword` when `**password_mechanism**` set to `ssh_askpass`.

_Used by:_ [ansible.builtin.ssh connection plugin](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/ssh_connection.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-ssh-connection)

ANSIBLE\_SU\_EXE[](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/environment_variables.html#envvar-ANSIBLE_SU_EXE "Link to this definition")

Su executable

_Used by:_ [ansible.builtin.su become plugin](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/su_become.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-su-become)

ANSIBLE\_SU\_FLAGS[](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/environment_variables.html#envvar-ANSIBLE_SU_FLAGS "Link to this definition")

Options to pass to su

_Used by:_ [ansible.builtin.su become plugin](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/su_become.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-su-become)

ANSIBLE\_SU\_PASS[](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/environment_variables.html#envvar-ANSIBLE_SU_PASS "Link to this definition")

Password to pass to su

_Used by:_ [ansible.builtin.su become plugin](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/su_become.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-su-become)

ANSIBLE\_SU\_PROMPT\_L10N[](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/environment_variables.html#envvar-ANSIBLE_SU_PROMPT_L10N "Link to this definition")

List of localized strings to match for prompt detection

If empty we’ll use the built in one

Do NOT add a colon (:) to your custom entries. Ansible adds a colon at the end of each prompt; if you add another one in your string, your prompt will fail with a “Timeout” error.

_Used by:_ [ansible.builtin.su become plugin](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/su_become.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-su-become)

ANSIBLE\_SU\_USER[](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/environment_variables.html#envvar-ANSIBLE_SU_USER "Link to this definition")

User you ‘become’ to execute the task

_Used by:_ [ansible.builtin.su become plugin](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/su_become.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-su-become)

ANSIBLE\_SUDO\_CHDIR[](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/environment_variables.html#envvar-ANSIBLE_SUDO_CHDIR "Link to this definition")

Directory to change to before invoking sudo; can avoid permission errors when dropping privileges.

_Used by:_ [ansible.builtin.sudo become plugin](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/sudo_become.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-sudo-become)

ANSIBLE\_SUDO\_EXE[](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/environment_variables.html#envvar-ANSIBLE_SUDO_EXE "Link to this definition")

Sudo executable

_Used by:_ [ansible.builtin.sudo become plugin](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/sudo_become.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-sudo-become)

ANSIBLE\_SUDO\_FLAGS[](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/environment_variables.html#envvar-ANSIBLE_SUDO_FLAGS "Link to this definition")

Options to pass to sudo

_Used by:_ [ansible.builtin.sudo become plugin](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/sudo_become.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-sudo-become)

ANSIBLE\_SUDO\_PASS[](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/environment_variables.html#envvar-ANSIBLE_SUDO_PASS "Link to this definition")

Password to pass to sudo

_Used by:_ [ansible.builtin.sudo become plugin](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/sudo_become.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-sudo-become)

ANSIBLE\_SUDO\_USER[](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/environment_variables.html#envvar-ANSIBLE_SUDO_USER "Link to this definition")

User you ‘become’ to execute the task

_Used by:_ [ansible.builtin.sudo become plugin](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/sudo_become.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-sudo-become)

ANSIBLE\_SYSTEM\_TMPDIRS[](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/environment_variables.html#envvar-ANSIBLE_SYSTEM_TMPDIRS "Link to this definition")

List of valid system temporary directories on the managed machine for Ansible to validate `**remote_tmp**` against, when specific permissions are needed. These must be world readable, writable, and executable. This list should only contain directories which the system administrator has pre-created with the proper ownership and permissions otherwise security issues can arise.

When `**remote_tmp**` is required to be a system temp dir and it does not match any in the list, the first one from the list will be used instead.

_Used by:_ [ansible.builtin.sh shell plugin](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/sh_shell.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-sh-shell)

ANSIBLE\_VARS\_PLUGIN\_STAGE[](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/environment_variables.html#envvar-ANSIBLE_VARS_PLUGIN_STAGE "Link to this definition")

Control when this vars plugin may be executed.

Setting this option to `all` will run the vars plugin after importing inventory and whenever it is demanded by a task.

Setting this option to `task` will only run the vars plugin whenever it is demanded by a task.

Setting this option to `inventory` will only run the vars plugin after parsing inventory.

If this option is omitted, the global `RUN_VARS_PLUGINS` configuration is used to determine when to execute the vars plugin.

_Used by:_ [ansible.builtin.host\_group\_vars vars plugin](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/host_group_vars_vars.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-host-group-vars-vars)

JUNIT\_FAIL\_ON\_CHANGE[](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/environment_variables.html#envvar-JUNIT_FAIL_ON_CHANGE "Link to this definition")

Consider any tasks reporting “changed” as a junit test failure

_Used by:_ [ansible.builtin.junit callback plugin](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/junit_callback.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-junit-callback)

JUNIT\_FAIL\_ON\_IGNORE[](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/environment_variables.html#envvar-JUNIT_FAIL_ON_IGNORE "Link to this definition")

Consider failed tasks as a junit test failure even if ignore\_on\_error is set

_Used by:_ [ansible.builtin.junit callback plugin](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/junit_callback.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-junit-callback)

JUNIT\_HIDE\_TASK\_ARGUMENTS[](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/environment_variables.html#envvar-JUNIT_HIDE_TASK_ARGUMENTS "Link to this definition")

Hide the arguments for a task

_Used by:_ [ansible.builtin.junit callback plugin](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/junit_callback.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-junit-callback)

JUNIT\_INCLUDE\_SETUP\_TASKS\_IN\_REPORT[](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/environment_variables.html#envvar-JUNIT_INCLUDE_SETUP_TASKS_IN_REPORT "Link to this definition")

Should the setup tasks be included in the final report

_Used by:_ [ansible.builtin.junit callback plugin](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/junit_callback.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-junit-callback)

JUNIT\_OUTPUT\_DIR[](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/environment_variables.html#envvar-JUNIT_OUTPUT_DIR "Link to this definition")

Directory to write XML files to.

_Used by:_ [ansible.builtin.junit callback plugin](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/junit_callback.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-junit-callback)

JUNIT\_REPLACE\_OUT\_OF\_TREE\_PATH[](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/environment_variables.html#envvar-JUNIT_REPLACE_OUT_OF_TREE_PATH "Link to this definition")

Replace the directory portion of an out-of-tree relative task path with the given placeholder

_Used by:_ [ansible.builtin.junit callback plugin](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/junit_callback.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-junit-callback)

JUNIT\_TASK\_CLASS[](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/environment_variables.html#envvar-JUNIT_TASK_CLASS "Link to this definition")

Configure the output to be one class per yaml file

_Used by:_ [ansible.builtin.junit callback plugin](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/junit_callback.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-junit-callback)

JUNIT\_TASK\_RELATIVE\_PATH[](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/environment_variables.html#envvar-JUNIT_TASK_RELATIVE_PATH "Link to this definition")

Configure the output to use relative paths to given directory

_Used by:_ [ansible.builtin.junit callback plugin](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/junit_callback.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-junit-callback)

JUNIT\_TEST\_CASE\_PREFIX[](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/environment_variables.html#envvar-JUNIT_TEST_CASE_PREFIX "Link to this definition")

Consider a task only as test case if it has this value as prefix. Additionally failing tasks are recorded as failed test cases.

_Used by:_ [ansible.builtin.junit callback plugin](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/junit_callback.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-junit-callback)
