*   [jsonfile cache](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/jsonfile_cache.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-jsonfile-cache)
     – JSON formatted files.
    
*   [memory cache](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/memory_cache.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-memory-cache)
     – RAM backed, non persistent
    

### Callback Plugins[](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/index.html#callback-plugins "Link to this heading")

*   [default callback](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/default_callback.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-default-callback)
     – default Ansible screen output
    
*   [junit callback](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/junit_callback.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-junit-callback)
     – write playbook output to a JUnit file.
    
*   [minimal callback](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/minimal_callback.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-minimal-callback)
     – minimal Ansible screen output
    
*   [oneline callback](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/oneline_callback.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-oneline-callback)
     – oneline Ansible screen output
    
*   [tree callback](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/tree_callback.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-tree-callback)
     – Save host events to files
    

### Connection Plugins[](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/index.html#connection-plugins "Link to this heading")

*   [local connection](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/local_connection.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-local-connection)
     – execute on controller
    
*   [paramiko\_ssh connection](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/paramiko_ssh_connection.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-paramiko-ssh-connection)
     – Run tasks via Python SSH (paramiko)
    
*   [psrp connection](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/psrp_connection.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-psrp-connection)
     – Run tasks over Microsoft PowerShell Remoting Protocol
    
*   [ssh connection](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/ssh_connection.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-ssh-connection)
     – connect via SSH client binary
    
*   [winrm connection](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/winrm_connection.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-winrm-connection)
     – Run tasks over Microsoft’s WinRM
    

### Filter Plugins[](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/index.html#filter-plugins "Link to this heading")

*   [abs filter](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/abs_filter.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-abs-filter)
     – Return the absolute value of the argument.
    
*   [attr filter](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/attr_filter.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-attr-filter)
     – Get an attribute of an object.
    
*   [b64decode filter](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/b64decode_filter.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-b64decode-filter)
     – Decode a Base64 string
    
*   [b64encode filter](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/b64encode_filter.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-b64encode-filter)
     – Encode a string as Base64
    
*   [basename filter](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/basename_filter.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-basename-filter)
     – get a path’s base name
    
*   [batch filter](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/batch_filter.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-batch-filter)
     – A filter that batches items.
    
*   [bool filter](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/bool_filter.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-bool-filter)
     – coerce some well-known truthy/falsy values to a boolean
    
*   [capitalize filter](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/capitalize_filter.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-capitalize-filter)
     – Capitalize a value.
    
*   [center filter](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/center_filter.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-center-filter)
     – Centers the value in a field of a given width.
    
*   [checksum filter](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/checksum_filter.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-checksum-filter)
     – checksum of input data
    
*   [combinations filter](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/combinations_filter.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-combinations-filter)
     – combinations from the elements of a list
    
*   [combine filter](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/combine_filter.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-combine-filter)
     – combine two dictionaries
    
*   [comment filter](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/comment_filter.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-comment-filter)
     – comment out a string
    
*   [commonpath filter](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/commonpath_filter.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-commonpath-filter)
     – gets the common path
    
*   [count filter](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/count_filter.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-count-filter)
     – Return the number of items in a container.
    
*   [d filter](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/d_filter.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-d-filter)
     – If the value is undefined it will return the passed default value, otherwise the value of the variable.
    
*   [default filter](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/default_filter.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-default-filter)
     – If the value is undefined it will return the passed default value, otherwise the value of the variable.
    
*   [dict2items filter](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/dict2items_filter.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-dict2items-filter)
     – Convert a dictionary into an itemized list of dictionaries
    
*   [dictsort filter](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/dictsort_filter.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-dictsort-filter)
     – Sort a dict and yield.
    
*   [difference filter](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/difference_filter.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-difference-filter)
     – the difference of one list from another
    
*   [dirname filter](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/dirname_filter.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-dirname-filter)
     – get a path’s directory name
    
*   [e filter](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/e_filter.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-e-filter)
     – Replace the characters \`\`&\`\`, \`\`<\`\`, \`\`>\`\`, \`\`’\`\`, and \`\`”\`\` in the string with HTML-safe sequences.
    
*   [escape filter](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/escape_filter.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-escape-filter)
     – Replace the characters \`\`&\`\`, \`\`<\`\`, \`\`>\`\`, \`\`’\`\`, and \`\`”\`\` in the string with HTML-safe sequences.
    
*   [expanduser filter](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/expanduser_filter.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-expanduser-filter)
     – Returns a path with `~` translation.
    
*   [expandvars filter](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/expandvars_filter.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-expandvars-filter)
     – expand environment variables
    
*   [extract filter](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/extract_filter.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-extract-filter)
     – extract a value based on an index or key
    
*   [fileglob filter](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/fileglob_filter.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-fileglob-filter)
     – explode a path glob to matching files
    
*   [filesizeformat filter](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/filesizeformat_filter.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-filesizeformat-filter)
     – Format the value like a ‘human-readable’ file size.
    
*   [first filter](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/first_filter.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-first-filter)
     – Return the first item of a sequence.
    
*   [flatten filter](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/flatten_filter.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-flatten-filter)
     – flatten lists within a list
    
*   [float filter](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/float_filter.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-float-filter)
     – Convert the value into a floating point number.
    
*   [forceescape filter](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/forceescape_filter.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-forceescape-filter)
     – Enforce HTML escaping.
    
*   [format filter](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/format_filter.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-format-filter)
     – Apply the given values to a \`printf-style\`\_ format string, like \`\`string % values\`\`.
    
*   [from\_json filter](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/from_json_filter.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-from-json-filter)
     – Convert JSON string into variable structure
    
*   [from\_yaml filter](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/from_yaml_filter.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-from-yaml-filter)
     – Convert YAML string into variable structure
    
*   [from\_yaml\_all filter](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/from_yaml_all_filter.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-from-yaml-all-filter)
     – Convert a series of YAML documents into a variable structure
    
*   [groupby filter](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/groupby_filter.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-groupby-filter)
     – Group a sequence of objects by an attribute using Python’s :func:\`itertools.
    
*   [hash filter](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/hash_filter.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-hash-filter)
     – hash of input data
    
*   [human\_readable filter](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/human_readable_filter.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-human-readable-filter)
     – Make bytes/bits human-readable
    
*   [human\_to\_bytes filter](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/human_to_bytes_filter.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-human-to-bytes-filter)
     – Get bytes from string
    
*   [indent filter](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/indent_filter.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-indent-filter)
     – Return a copy of the string with each line indented by 4 spaces.
    
*   [int filter](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/int_filter.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-int-filter)
     – Convert the value into an integer.
    
*   [intersect filter](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/intersect_filter.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-intersect-filter)
     – intersection of lists
    
*   [items filter](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/items_filter.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-items-filter)
     – Return an iterator over the \`\`(key, value)\`\` items of a mapping.
    
*   [items2dict filter](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/items2dict_filter.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-items2dict-filter)
     – Consolidate a list of itemized dictionaries into a dictionary
    
*   [join filter](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/join_filter.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-join-filter)
     – Return a string which is the concatenation of the strings in the sequence.
    
*   [last filter](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/last_filter.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-last-filter)
     – Return the last item of a sequence.
    
*   [length filter](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/length_filter.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-length-filter)
     – Return the number of items in a container.
    
*   [list filter](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/list_filter.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-list-filter)
     – Convert the value into a list.
    
*   [log filter](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/log_filter.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-log-filter)
     – log of (math operation)
    
*   [lower filter](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/lower_filter.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-lower-filter)
     – Convert a value to lowercase.
    
*   [mandatory filter](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/mandatory_filter.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-mandatory-filter)
     – make a variable’s existence mandatory
    
*   [map filter](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/map_filter.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-map-filter)
     – Applies a filter on a sequence of objects or looks up an attribute.
    
*   [max filter](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/max_filter.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-max-filter)
     – Return the largest item from the sequence.
    
*   [md5 filter](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/md5_filter.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-md5-filter)
     – MD5 hash of input data
    
*   [min filter](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/min_filter.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-min-filter)
     – Return the smallest item from the sequence.
    
*   [normpath filter](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/normpath_filter.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-normpath-filter)
     – Normalize a pathname
    
*   [password\_hash filter](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/password_hash_filter.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-password-hash-filter)
     – convert input password into password\_hash
    
*   [path\_join filter](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/path_join_filter.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-path-join-filter)
     – Join one or more path components
    
*   [permutations filter](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/permutations_filter.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-permutations-filter)
     – permutations from the elements of a list
    
*   [pow filter](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/pow_filter.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-pow-filter)
     – power of (math operation)
    
*   [pprint filter](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/pprint_filter.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-pprint-filter)
     – Pretty print a variable.
    
*   [product filter](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/product_filter.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-product-filter)
     – cartesian product of lists
    
*   [quote filter](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/quote_filter.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-quote-filter)
     – shell quoting
    
*   [random filter](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/random_filter.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-random-filter)
     – random number or list item
    
*   [realpath filter](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/realpath_filter.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-realpath-filter)
     – Turn path into real path
    
*   [regex\_escape filter](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/regex_escape_filter.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-regex-escape-filter)
     – escape regex chars
    
*   [regex\_findall filter](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/regex_findall_filter.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-regex-findall-filter)
     – extract all regex matches from string
    
*   [regex\_replace filter](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/regex_replace_filter.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-regex-replace-filter)
     – replace a string via regex
    
*   [regex\_search filter](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/regex_search_filter.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-regex-search-filter)
     – extract regex match from string
    
*   [reject filter](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/reject_filter.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-reject-filter)
     – Filters a sequence of objects by applying a test to each object, and rejecting the objects with the test succeeding.
    
*   [rejectattr filter](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/rejectattr_filter.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-rejectattr-filter)
     – Filters a sequence of objects by applying a test to the specified attribute of each object, and rejecting the objects with the test succeeding.
    
*   [rekey\_on\_member filter](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/rekey_on_member_filter.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-rekey-on-member-filter)
     – Rekey a list of dicts into a dict using a member
    
*   [relpath filter](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/relpath_filter.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-relpath-filter)
     – Make a path relative
    
*   [replace filter](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/replace_filter.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-replace-filter)
     – Return a copy of the value with all occurrences of a substring replaced with a new one.
    
*   [reverse filter](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/reverse_filter.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-reverse-filter)
     – Reverse the object or return an iterator that iterates over it the other way round.
    
*   [root filter](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/root_filter.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-root-filter)
     – root of (math operation)
    
*   [round filter](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/round_filter.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-round-filter)
     – Round the number to a given precision.
    
*   [safe filter](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/safe_filter.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-safe-filter)
     – Mark the value as safe which means that in an environment with automatic escaping enabled this variable will not be escaped.
    
*   [select filter](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/select_filter.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-select-filter)
     – Filters a sequence of objects by applying a test to each object, and only selecting the objects with the test succeeding.
    
*   [selectattr filter](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/selectattr_filter.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-selectattr-filter)
     – Filters a sequence of objects by applying a test to the specified attribute of each object, and only selecting the objects with the test succeeding.
    
*   [sha1 filter](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/sha1_filter.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-sha1-filter)
     – SHA-1 hash of input data
    
*   [shuffle filter](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/shuffle_filter.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-shuffle-filter)
     – randomize a list
    
*   [slice filter](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/slice_filter.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-slice-filter)
     – Slice an iterator and return a list of lists containing those items.
    
*   [sort filter](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/sort_filter.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-sort-filter)
     – Sort an iterable using Python’s :func:\`sorted\`.
    
*   [split filter](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/split_filter.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-split-filter)
     – split a string into a list
    
*   [splitext filter](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/splitext_filter.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-splitext-filter)
     – split a path into root and file extension
    
*   [strftime filter](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/strftime_filter.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-strftime-filter)
     – Returns date and/or time
    
*   [string filter](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/string_filter.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-string-filter)
     – Convert an object to a string if it isn’t already.
    
*   [striptags filter](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/striptags_filter.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-striptags-filter)
     – Strip SGML/XML tags and replace adjacent whitespace by one space.
    
*   [subelements filter](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/subelements_filter.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-subelements-filter)
     – returns a product of a list and its elements
    
*   [sum filter](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/sum_filter.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-sum-filter)
     – Returns the sum of a sequence of numbers plus the value of parameter ‘start’.
    
*   [symmetric\_difference filter](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/symmetric_difference_filter.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-symmetric-difference-filter)
     – different items from two lists
    
*   [ternary filter](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/ternary_filter.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-ternary-filter)
     – Ternary operation filter
    
*   [title filter](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/title_filter.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-title-filter)
     – Return a titlecased version of the value.
    
*   [to\_datetime filter](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/to_datetime_filter.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-to-datetime-filter)
     – Get `datetime` from string
    
*   [to\_json filter](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/to_json_filter.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-to-json-filter)
     – Convert variable to JSON string
    
*   [to\_nice\_json filter](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/to_nice_json_filter.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-to-nice-json-filter)
     – Convert variable to ‘nicely formatted’ JSON string
    
*   [to\_nice\_yaml filter](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/to_nice_yaml_filter.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-to-nice-yaml-filter)
     – Convert variable to YAML string
    
*   [to\_uuid filter](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/to_uuid_filter.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-to-uuid-filter)
     – namespaced UUID generator
    
*   [to\_yaml filter](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/to_yaml_filter.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-to-yaml-filter)
     – Convert variable to YAML string
    
*   [tojson filter](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/tojson_filter.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-tojson-filter)
     – Serialize an object to a string of JSON, and mark it safe to render in HTML.
    
*   [trim filter](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/trim_filter.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-trim-filter)
     – Strip leading and trailing characters, by default whitespace.
    
*   [truncate filter](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/truncate_filter.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-truncate-filter)
     – Return a truncated copy of the string.
    
*   [type\_debug filter](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/type_debug_filter.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-type-debug-filter)
     – show input data type
    
*   [union filter](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/union_filter.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-union-filter)
     – union of lists
    
*   [unique filter](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/unique_filter.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-unique-filter)
     – set of unique items of a list
    
*   [unvault filter](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/unvault_filter.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-unvault-filter)
     – Open an Ansible Vault
    
*   [upper filter](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/upper_filter.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-upper-filter)
     – Convert a value to uppercase.
    
*   [urldecode filter](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/urldecode_filter.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-urldecode-filter)
     – Decode percent-encoded sequences
    
*   [urlencode filter](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/urlencode_filter.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-urlencode-filter)
     – Quote data for use in a URL path or query using UTF-8.
    
*   [urlize filter](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/urlize_filter.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-urlize-filter)
     – Convert URLs in text into clickable links.
    
*   [urlsplit filter](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/urlsplit_filter.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-urlsplit-filter)
     – get components from URL
    
*   [vault filter](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/vault_filter.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-vault-filter)
     – vault your secrets
    
*   [win\_basename filter](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/win_basename_filter.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-win-basename-filter)
     – Get a Windows path’s base name
    
*   [win\_dirname filter](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/win_dirname_filter.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-win-dirname-filter)
     – Get a Windows path’s directory
    
*   [win\_splitdrive filter](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/win_splitdrive_filter.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-win-splitdrive-filter)
     – Split a Windows path by the drive letter
    
*   [wordcount filter](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/wordcount_filter.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-wordcount-filter)
     – Count the words in that string.
    
*   [wordwrap filter](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/wordwrap_filter.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-wordwrap-filter)
     – Wrap a string to the given width.
    
*   [xmlattr filter](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/xmlattr_filter.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-xmlattr-filter)
     – Create an SGML/XML attribute string based on the items in a dict.
    
*   [zip filter](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/zip_filter.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-zip-filter)
     – combine list elements
    
*   [zip\_longest filter](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/zip_longest_filter.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-zip-longest-filter)
     – combine list elements, with filler
    

### Inventory Plugins[](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/index.html#inventory-plugins "Link to this heading")

*   [advanced\_host\_list inventory](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/advanced_host_list_inventory.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-advanced-host-list-inventory)
     – Parses a ‘host list’ with ranges
    
*   [auto inventory](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/auto_inventory.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-auto-inventory)
     – Loads and executes an inventory plugin specified in a YAML config
    
*   [constructed inventory](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/constructed_inventory.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-constructed-inventory)
     – Uses Jinja2 to construct vars and groups based on existing inventory.
    
*   [generator inventory](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/generator_inventory.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-generator-inventory)
     – Uses Jinja2 to construct hosts and groups from patterns
    
*   [host\_list inventory](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/host_list_inventory.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-host-list-inventory)
     – Parses a ‘host list’ string
    
*   [ini inventory](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/ini_inventory.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-ini-inventory)
     – Uses an Ansible INI file as inventory source.
    
*   [script inventory](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/script_inventory.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-script-inventory)
     – Executes an inventory script that returns JSON
    
*   [toml inventory](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/toml_inventory.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-toml-inventory)
     – Uses a specific TOML file as an inventory source.
    
*   [yaml inventory](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/yaml_inventory.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-yaml-inventory)
     – Uses a specific YAML file as an inventory source.
    

### Lookup Plugins[](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/index.html#lookup-plugins "Link to this heading")

*   [config lookup](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/config_lookup.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-config-lookup)
     – Display the ‘resolved’ Ansible option values.
    
*   [csvfile lookup](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/csvfile_lookup.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-csvfile-lookup)
     – read data from a TSV or CSV file
    
*   [dict lookup](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/dict_lookup.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-dict-lookup)
     – returns key/value pair items from dictionaries
    
*   [env lookup](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/env_lookup.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-env-lookup)
     – Read the value of environment variables
    
*   [file lookup](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/file_lookup.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-file-lookup)
     – read file contents
    
*   [fileglob lookup](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/fileglob_lookup.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-fileglob-lookup)
     – list files matching a pattern
    
*   [first\_found lookup](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/first_found_lookup.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-first-found-lookup)
     – return first file found from list
    
*   [indexed\_items lookup](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/indexed_items_lookup.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-indexed-items-lookup)
     – rewrites lists to return ‘indexed items’
    
*   [ini lookup](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/ini_lookup.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-ini-lookup)
     – read data from an ini file
    
*   [inventory\_hostnames lookup](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/inventory_hostnames_lookup.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-inventory-hostnames-lookup)
     – list of inventory hosts matching a host pattern
    
*   [items lookup](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/items_lookup.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-items-lookup)
     – list of items
    
*   [lines lookup](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/lines_lookup.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-lines-lookup)
     – read lines from command
    
*   [list lookup](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/list_lookup.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-list-lookup)
     – simply returns what it is given.
    
*   [nested lookup](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/nested_lookup.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-nested-lookup)
     – composes a list with nested elements of other lists
    
*   [password lookup](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/password_lookup.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-password-lookup)
     – retrieve or generate a random password, stored in a file
    
*   [pipe lookup](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/pipe_lookup.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-pipe-lookup)
     – read output from a command
    
*   [random\_choice lookup](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/random_choice_lookup.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-random-choice-lookup)
     – return random element from list
    
*   [sequence lookup](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/sequence_lookup.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-sequence-lookup)
     – generate a list based on a number sequence
    
*   [subelements lookup](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/subelements_lookup.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-subelements-lookup)
     – traverse nested key from a list of dictionaries
    
*   [template lookup](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/template_lookup.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-template-lookup)
     – retrieve contents of file after templating with Jinja2
    
*   [together lookup](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/together_lookup.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-together-lookup)
     – merges lists into synchronized list
    
*   [unvault lookup](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/unvault_lookup.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-unvault-lookup)
     – read vaulted file(s) contents
    
*   [url lookup](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/url_lookup.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-url-lookup)
     – return contents from URL
    
*   [varnames lookup](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/varnames_lookup.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-varnames-lookup)
     – Lookup matching variable names
    
*   [vars lookup](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/vars_lookup.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-vars-lookup)
     – Lookup templated value of variables
    

### Shell Plugins[](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/index.html#shell-plugins "Link to this heading")

*   [cmd shell](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/cmd_shell.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-cmd-shell)
     – Windows Command Prompt
    
*   [powershell shell](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/powershell_shell.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-powershell-shell)
     – Windows PowerShell
    
*   [sh shell](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/sh_shell.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-sh-shell)
     – POSIX shell (/bin/sh)
    

### Strategy Plugins[](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/index.html#strategy-plugins "Link to this heading")

*   [debug strategy](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/debug_strategy.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-debug-strategy)
     – Executes tasks in interactive debug session.
    
*   [free strategy](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/free_strategy.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-free-strategy)
     – Executes tasks without waiting for all hosts
    
*   [host\_pinned strategy](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/host_pinned_strategy.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-host-pinned-strategy)
     – Executes tasks on each host without interruption
    
*   [linear strategy](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/linear_strategy.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-linear-strategy)
     – Executes tasks in a linear fashion
    

### Test Plugins[](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/index.html#test-plugins "Link to this heading")

*   [abs test](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/abs_test.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-abs-test)
     – is the path absolute
    
*   [all test](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/all_test.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-all-test)
     – are all conditions in a list true
    
*   [any test](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/any_test.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-any-test)
     – is any condition in a list true
    
*   [boolean test](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/boolean_test.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-boolean-test)
     – Return true if the object is a boolean value.
    
*   [callable test](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/callable_test.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-callable-test)
     – Return whether the object is callable.
    
*   [changed test](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/changed_test.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-changed-test)
     – did the task require changes
    
*   [contains test](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/contains_test.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-contains-test)
     – does the list contain this element
    
*   [defined test](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/defined_test.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-defined-test)
     – Return true if the variable is defined.
    
*   [directory test](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/directory_test.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-directory-test)
     – does the path resolve to an existing directory
    
*   [divisibleby test](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/divisibleby_test.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-divisibleby-test)
     – Check if a variable is divisible by a number.
    
*   [eq test](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/eq_test.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-eq-test)
     – Same as a == b.
    
*   [equalto test](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/equalto_test.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-equalto-test)
     – Same as a == b.
    
*   [escaped test](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/escaped_test.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-escaped-test)
     – Check if the value is escaped.
    
*   [even test](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/even_test.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-even-test)
     – Return true if the variable is even.
    
*   [exists test](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/exists_test.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-exists-test)
     – does the path exist, follow symlinks
    
*   [failed test](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/failed_test.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-failed-test)
     – did the task fail
    
*   [false test](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/false_test.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-false-test)
     – Return true if the object is False.
    
*   [falsy test](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/falsy_test.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-falsy-test)
     – Pythonic false
    
*   [file test](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/file_test.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-file-test)
     – does the path resolve to an existing file
    
*   [filter test](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/filter_test.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-filter-test)
     – Check if a filter exists by name.
    
*   [finished test](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/finished_test.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-finished-test)
     – Did async task finish
    
*   [float test](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/float_test.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-float-test)
     – Return true if the object is a float.
    
*   [ge test](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/ge_test.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-ge-test)
     – Same as a >= b.
    
*   [greaterthan test](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/greaterthan_test.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-greaterthan-test)
     – Same as a > b.
    
*   [gt test](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/gt_test.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-gt-test)
     – Same as a > b.
    
*   [in test](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/in_test.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-in-test)
     – Check if value is in seq.
    
*   [integer test](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/integer_test.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-integer-test)
     – Return true if the object is an integer.
    
*   [iterable test](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/iterable_test.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-iterable-test)
     – Check if it’s possible to iterate over an object.
    
*   [le test](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/le_test.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-le-test)
     – Same as a <= b.
    
*   [lessthan test](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/lessthan_test.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-lessthan-test)
     – Same as a < b.
    
*   [link test](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/link_test.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-link-test)
     – does the path reference existing symbolic link
    
*   [link\_exists test](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/link_exists_test.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-link-exists-test)
     – does the path exist, no follow
    
*   [lower test](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/lower_test.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-lower-test)
     – Return true if the variable is lowercased.
    
*   [lt test](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/lt_test.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-lt-test)
     – Same as a < b.
    
*   [mapping test](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/mapping_test.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-mapping-test)
     – Return true if the object is a mapping.
    
*   [match test](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/match_test.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-match-test)
     – Does string match regular expression from the start
    
*   [mount test](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/mount_test.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-mount-test)
     – does the path resolve to mount point
    
*   [nan test](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/nan_test.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-nan-test)
     – is this not a number (NaN)
    
*   [ne test](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/ne_test.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-ne-test)
     – Same as a .
    
*   [none test](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/none_test.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-none-test)
     – Return true if the variable is none.
    
*   [number test](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/number_test.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-number-test)
     – Return true if the variable is a number.
    
*   [odd test](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/odd_test.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-odd-test)
     – Return true if the variable is odd.
    
*   [reachable test](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/reachable_test.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-reachable-test)
     – Task did not end due to unreachable host
    
*   [regex test](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/regex_test.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-regex-test)
     – Does string match regular expression from the start
    
*   [same\_file test](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/same_file_test.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-same-file-test)
     – compares two paths to see if they resolve to the same filesystem object
    
*   [sameas test](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/sameas_test.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-sameas-test)
     – Check if an object points to the same memory address than another object.
    
*   [search test](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/search_test.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-search-test)
     – Does string match a regular expression
    
*   [sequence test](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/sequence_test.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-sequence-test)
     – Return true if the variable is a sequence.
    
*   [skipped test](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/skipped_test.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-skipped-test)
     – Was task skipped
    
*   [started test](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/started_test.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-started-test)
     – Was async task started
    
*   [string test](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/string_test.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-string-test)
     – Return true if the object is a string.
    
*   [subset test](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/subset_test.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-subset-test)
     – is the list a subset of this other list
    
*   [success test](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/success_test.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-success-test)
     – check task success
    
*   [superset test](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/superset_test.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-superset-test)
     – is the list a superset of this other list
    
*   [test test](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/test_test.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-test-test)
     – Check if a test exists by name.
    
*   [timedout test](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/timedout_test.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-timedout-test)
     – did the task time out
    
*   [true test](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/true_test.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-true-test)
     – Return true if the object is True.
    
*   [truthy test](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/truthy_test.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-truthy-test)
     – Pythonic true
    
*   [undefined test](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/undefined_test.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-undefined-test)
     – Like :func:\`defined\` but the other way round.
    
*   [unreachable test](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/unreachable_test.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-unreachable-test)
     – Did task end due to the host was unreachable
    
*   [upper test](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/upper_test.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-upper-test)
     – Return true if the variable is uppercased.
    
*   [uri test](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/uri_test.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-uri-test)
     – is the string a valid URI
    
*   [url test](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/url_test.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-url-test)
     – is the string a valid URL
    
*   [urn test](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/urn_test.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-urn-test)
     – is the string a valid URN
    
*   [vault\_encrypted test](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/vault_encrypted_test.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-vault-encrypted-test)
     – Is this an encrypted vault string
    
*   [vaulted\_file test](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/vaulted_file_test.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-vaulted-file-test)
     – Is this file an encrypted vault
    
*   [version test](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/version_test.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-version-test)
     – compare version strings
    

### Vars Plugins[](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/index.html#vars-plugins "Link to this heading")

*   [host\_group\_vars vars](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/ansible/builtin/host_group_vars_vars.html#ansible-collections-ansible-builtin-host-group-vars-vars)
     – In charge of loading group\_vars and host\_vars
    

See also

List of [collections](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible-core/devel/collections/index.html#list-of-collections)
 with docs hosted here.
